not sure what the different meters on some of these tooltips mean. need to ask.
When shown on an Armor item, the bar represents DURABILITY
When shown on a Weapon item, the bar represents CHARGE

Weapons
	- normal items dont have charge, so they dont show a meter
	- magical/legendary/artifact items have charge, so they show a meter

Armor
	- all armor items show a durability meter

2013-09-12 14:59:59
Layout v1 observations:
The clustering of information isnt quite right. Here's what I've done:
Classification
	P2	Item type
	P2	Item binding status
Identification
	P1	Item Name
	P1	Primary Stats
Details
	P2	Additional item details



By comparison, the existing item tooltips in ESO info cluster looks like this:
Classification
	P2	Item type
	P2	Item binding status
Identification
	P1	Item name
Details
	P1	Primary Stats
	P2	Additional item details


By comparison the info clustering for Skyrim looks like this:
Identification
	P1	Item name
Details
	P1	Primary Stats
	P2	Additional item    details

or something along these lines... :)
Next Steps:
- reduce whitespace between primary stats and additional item details
- dont use a double horizontal rule treatment. This is placing too much emphasis on the primary stats
- explore replacing horizontal rule with charge/duration meter
- explore placing duration meter in upper R, while placing binding status underneath item type on left hand side

2013-09-13 16:39:07
Spoke w Ed about Enchanted Items and Set items

ENCHANTMENTS
	- Only 1 enchantment can be applied to an item

TRAITS
	- new system, just designed, no example in game
	- Only 1 trait can be applied to an item

SETS
	- Existing implementation in game is bafflingly phrased, so fix that
	- A set consistes of several items
	- There is a table of bonuses for completing part of or all of the set
		- its currently conveyed bafflingly in the tooltip, so fix that
	- The bonus table is an effect list with multiple conditions
		- instead of:
			(on Equip)
				- Effect 1
				- Effect 2

		- instead of:
			(on Use)
				- Effect 1

		Its like this:
			(on own 3 of 7 items)
				- Effect 1
			(on own 7 of 7 items)
				- Effect 2

	Example:

		I own 4/7 items in the Archmage Set
		The bonus table is an effect list whose items become active

		Robe of The Arch Mage
		Part of the Arch Mage Set

2013-09-17 17:49:48
Looking into inventory item comparison. There's always a tempation to be verbose with these, and in fact, some do. For our purposes though, minimalism is the key.
- EQUIPPED text doesnt need to be huge, but it needs to be placed so its easily visible and uncluttered
- EQUIPPED text doesnt need further qualifiers (main hand/off hand)
- Ideally, tooltip placement should reflect player equip slot placement for maximum affordance. However it doesnt seem to me that this will be possible given available screen real estate at minimum resolution

2013-09-22 20:10:16
looking through these ability tooltip templates, the following use cases present themselves:

ACTIVE & ULTIMATE ABILITIES

	ABILITY LOCKED
	-	Unlocked at a higher rank

	ABILITY UNAVAILABLE
	-	Not enough skill points to purchase ability

	ABILITY AVAILABLE
	-	Ability has hasnt been purchased
	-	Skill points available

	ABILITY ACTIVE
	-	Ability is increasing with character level

2013-09-25 10:30:22
WTF. Fine tuned PDF export workflow.. Will re-export later



ABILITY TOOLTIP FEEDBACK v2

ISSUE1
(a) The ability progression experience bar is inconsistent between the mouseover tooltip and the skill line progression. Seeing 2 differently stylized elements that represent the same thing seems weird to me.

(b) we can’t currently do a status bar with double leading edges (the slant on both sides) unless it is center aligned. There are tech limitations which we probably will not have the time left to deal with. If the blue part were centered in the middle that would work, or else it will need to have a slant on just one side or the other.

(c) Some tooltips have the pin line and some have the experience bar. In which cases do I see each?

(d) Does the XP bar go away once the skill is maxed?

	RESPONSE:
		(a) As per the current implementation, The progress meter for an ability tooltip only appears when hovering over the ability bar

		(b) When the skill is maxed, the meter isnt drawn
		(c) I get what this feature is about, but am not enthusiastic about it
			(1) it segments Ultimate/Active Ability tooltips into 2 types (1 drawn next to abilities in the list, the other drawn for abilities on the ability bar)
			(2) the type of tooltip drawn on the ability bar special cases when an ability is maxed, and doesnt draw a progress meter
			(3) As Lee mentioned in his feedback (paraphrasing) the meter on the tooltip reads like it represents "charge" rather than "progression". After all, we use a similar meter to represent charge & durability for items.
		(d) My suggestion is that clicking on an Ability in the Ability bar can refresh the list so that it opens to the section where that ability is contained. This would allow the player to see progression in the context where its already most visible. We'd need to include logic to isolate behavior like this from cases where the player is simply dragging & dropping abilities

		<INCLUDE SIMPLE WIREFRAME SHOWING BEHAVIOR I DESCRIBED>

	ACTION
	-	create wireframe


ISSUE 3
For the frontal cone AoE, Using “Target: Area” seems a bit odd to me, since you don’t actually aim at a specific area as with GTAOE.  I’d suggest either “Target: Cone” or “Arc: 90”

	ACTION
	x	replace with "Target: Cone"

ISSUE 4
The conditional ability tooltip doesn’t strike me as the nicest looking thing in the world, I think because of the wrapped text.  Maybe we could break that into two lines?  Or maybe we could have a generic look for when there are multiple effects with the same duration.

	ACTION
	x	investigate layout possibility

	RESULT
	x	de-ephasized conditional text statement

ISSUE 5
Andrew didnt notice the Passive Ability progression use case because it was listed in the Use Cases section.

	ACTION
	x	copy this asset to the progression section

need to prepare gfx example of revised item tooltip and ability tooltip show at art review today in order to get the go ahead to move in this direction


ITEM TOOLTIP FEEDBACK
Dan mentions that we're possibly missing one use case:
	UNIQUENESS
		UNIQUE IN BAG
			can have only have one of these items in your inventory at any time
		UNIQUE EQUIPPED
			can only have one of these items equipped at any time

//
Why does weapon durability have a blue bar but armor has a brown bar? Is this because they should be repaired in different ways? This seems needlessly complicated and removes some of the uniformity between items.
Some of the Edge case items seem very far off from the “skyrim style” tooltips we were going for

	RESOLVED

// Floyd
Can we not do the contracting to the middle thing? I despise it on the health bar and really do not want to see it used anyplace else if it is at all avoidable.

// Lee
Were we not able to get the individual effect name on the potion tool tip?  I believe that will be important to the player and help reinforce when they use reagents with ‘Restore Health’ they get a potion with ‘Restore Health’.

	RESPONSE:
	we can. It was feeling a bit crowded to me, but you make a good point about associating the reagents with the end result. For sake of consistency, we'll want to do similar for vendor potions

	ACTION:
	update potions (crafted and vendor) to use header
	update runestone to use header for "translation"
	do an audit to find other cases where this type of header scheme can be used


// ERIC
I wonder if all of the constraints are still necessary:
-	Does the weapon type need to be in the upper right hand corner? It seems redundant to say “shield” when there’s an icon of a shield and it’s called “buckler of blocking”

-	Instead of saying “bound” in the upper left hand corner we could communicate that differently on the tooltip by adding a different border or icon or text color

-	I realize you lose some amount of clarity doing this but we have a popup message that occurs before you bind it so I don’t think people will have problems pairing the function to this visual language

-	The bonuses from an item set could be listed in your character sheet instead of on the item tooltip

-	I think the current mockups look great, but we could do more to achieve the game’s vision of a modern look

2013-10-09 10:00:15
writing up confluence page for Character Stats

What is it used for?
I go there to add points to my primary attributes (Magicka, Health, Stamina)
	-	I can look at the numeric values of my attributes and observe how they change when I add points to them
	-	I observe a high level overview of my character
		-	Name
		-	Race
		-	Class
		-	Alliance
		-	Title
		-	Attributes
			-	Magicka
			=	Magicka Recovery (combat)
			-	Health
			-	Health Recovery (combat)
			-	Stamina
			-	Stamina Recovery (combat)
			-	Spell Damage
			-	Spell Critical
			-	Spell Resistance
			-	Weapon Damage
			-	Weapon Critical
			-	Armor
		-	My Active mount
		-	Active Effects
		- 	Convey stat "overcharge" (link to stat overcharge doc)


Why do I want an overview of my character?
	-	The high level overview of my character helps me set goals for who I want that character to be and observe how changes to my circumstances affect those goals
	-	At every level there is a soft cap for all major attributes (health, armor, damage, etc.) that limits the effectiveness of single stat "stacking". This feature, called "overcharge" needs to be messaged to players when it happens.

What features does it have
-	add points to attributes from a pool of available points
-	page scrolling behavior allows the page design to maintain a fixed size while presenting an arbitrary amount of information

Its a bitter pill, but the reason I'm doing it this way is to focus on functionality and interactivity. This is the nature of the tool, so dont fight it

2013-10-22 09:39:30
menu feedback from Chris

-	Its your call. Do it the way you think works best
	-	my way works best


-	Widget states are indeterminate
	- OFF state appears like disabled

-	Where do the tooltips get drawn?

-	With the labels and widgets this far apart I find I am having to scan left to right to see a value. This is probably the biggest thing that I find to be a bit fatiguing.

-	There’s a gap between the section headers and the horizontal strip (where the description used to be). I think it just needs to scoot up a bit to match the header

-	We’re introducing some new concepts here that we don’t use elsewhere:
	-	We generally have dropdowns with the label above them, checkboxes with labels next to them, and radio buttons with the label above them.
	-	There’s a new widget type that is used for on/off. I’d like to avoid introducing a new widget at this point, since we’ve used standard ones throughout the rest of the UI.

CHRIS'S FEEDBACK

SLIDERS
-	Sliders no longer have numbers. We need to bring these back in all cases as it makes things a lot easier.

DROPDOWN
x	Dropdowns do not appear to be wide enough for some of the contents (like resolution). Could just be a font issue but this fit with the previous mockup?

HINTS
x	Center the Apply and Reset to Defaults keybind underneath the pane above it
x	We won’t need the ‘Alt’ to Exit button

VIDEO
x	move interface scaling/position to a new sub-section called Interface
x	add horizontal & vertical boundary sliders to this new Interface section
x	the Graphics Quality should feel spaced separately from the others just because we want this to feel like it’s the parent of all the children dropdowns below it
	x	indent (DD) sections beneath graphics quality
	x	For the Graphics Dropdowns, the order should be:
			Graphics Quality
			Texture Quality
			Subsampling Quality
			Shadow Quality
			Water Reflection Quality
			Particle Density

AUDIO
x	Audio setting should follow pattern of mockups
	(MUTE)(SLI) Master Volume
		(CB) Enable Sound
		(MUTE)(SLI) Environment Volume
		(MUTE)(SLI) Effects Volume
		(MUTE)(SLI) Conversation Volume
		(MUTE)(SLI) Interface Volume
		(CB) Enable Music
		(MUTE)(SLI) Music Volume

x	Add the following settings to Audio
		(SLI) Music Interval
		(DD) Sound Channels/Quality
			Lowest (16), Low (32), Medium (64), High (128)
		(DD) Sound Output/Audio Device
			Default, Speakers (ASUS XONAR Audio Device), Digital Audio (S/PDIF) (ASUS XONAR…)
		(DD) Speaker setup
			Default, Mono, Stereo, Quad, Surround, 5.1, 7.1, Pro Logic

CONTROLS

Working on add-ons UI revision

2013-10-27 18:04:49
add-ons are looking better. There's a choice to be made about how interactive it can be presented. Here's what I can't do:
-	make the addons switch on/off while allowing them to expand/contract and be sorted

However, can I sort and allow expand/contract?. After all - the on/off behavior is well documented elsewhere in the prototype

To sort & expand/contract:
I would need to create a dynamic panel that contained the sorted states of the list
Each of those states would contain a copy of the addons items and placement logic (for when list items expand and contract)

To turn on/off and expand/contract
I would need to use the existing structure
I'd need to add a state to the meta panel so it switches on/off
I'd need to create placement logic for expand/contract

Mark pointed out that loading screen needs "munge" gfx. Having hard time converting the dds files he'd referred me to however. I've replaced the placeholder gfx with the ones provided.


GAME MENUS ROUND 2 FEEDBACK


PRIVATE/PUBLIC ICONS ON LOGIN SCREEN
-	Change design so there's only one icon. When its toggled ON, you're in Trusted Mode. When its OFF you're in Public mode

x	When going from pre-login to char select, the menu on the left shifts.
	The top word in the menus should always be anchored vertically centered on the left, so that when you’re going back/forth the whole thing doesn’t shift up down and it starts at the same point.

x	Character label in the Game Menu on character select should be plural (Characters).

PRE-GAME MENU
x	Lose Gameplay, Interface & Social settings on Pregame menu
-	Add Apply, Reset Defaults hints to Pregame Menu settings
-	hide menu when going to credits
	- provide screen hint stating "(ESC) Exit Credits"

CHARACTER SELECT
x	Controls should be available on character select as well.


IN-GAME MENU
x	For the In game menu, Resume Game feels like it should be at the very top. This is in line with how we do the other menus since the top typically means move forward, the bottom means go back.

SLIDERS
x	All sliders should always have a value.

APPLY/RESET BUTTONS
-	These should show up in all cases where options & settings can be changed
-	Need to provide an UNDO CHANGES option

EDITORIAL
x	Any label that has more than one word (i.e. Environment volume) will need to have all the words capitalized. Just pointing this out as Editorial will hit us on this and change this anyways, even if we don’t.

LATENCY
-	Would only show up in the Game Menu when you’re in the game world, not during pre-game. It’s reversed currently in where it shows.
-	Would like an icon version replacing the ‘latency’ label if possible (see Borderlands 2) that is just a 3 bar indicator (green/yello/red as it changes). May be able to re-use the population bars from the Campaign Browser for AvA

VIDEO
x	Horizontal Boundary/Vertical boundary missing values for slider (0-100 are the values)
x	Get rid of "recommended" nomenclature from video settings dropdowns

AUDIO
x	Volume sliders need values (0 – 100 are the values).
x	Music Interval should be grouped under the Music section after Music Volume.
x	Music interval slider need values (in ms – not sure what the values would be yet as this concept doesn’t exist yet).

GAMEPLAY
x	Relabel Show Combat Tips to ‘Active Combat Tips’
x	Relabel Enable Double Tap to Dodge Roll to ‘Double Tap to Dodge’
x	Relabel the Loot section to ‘Items’
x	Add ‘Hide Helmet’ Toggle into here.
x	Relabel AoE Loot to ‘Consolidate Area Loot’

TARGETING
x	Need to move this over to the Indicators section of ‘Interface’ since it’s more in line with that area.

INTERFACE
	NAMEPLATES & HEALTHBARS
	x	These should be dropdowns versus the button labels
	x	Remove the ‘names’ suffix from these labels. Already implied as being part of nameplates. Pluralize the labels (Friendly NPCs, Friendly Players, etc.)

SOCIAL
	x	For Incoming Requests, remove the Ignore prefixes for the labels.
		Rename these labels in ALERTS section
		x	Contact comes online -> Contact Online
		x	Contact Goes offline -> Contact Offline
		x	Guild Member Comes Online -> Guild Member Online
		x	Guild Member Goes Offline -> Guild Member Offline

CHAT SETTINGS
	x	Change the text size to a slider (slider will just have locked sliding at increments, from 12 to 24 in increments of 2)

	Player Channel Colors and Guild Channel Colors:
	x	These should all just be under ‘Chat Colors’ and not separated out, since this grouping isn’t accurate. The ordering is good though
	-	Finalize Color Picker


CONTROLS
	CAMERA
	x	These should be available on Char Select as well.

	x	Camera needs to separate out the settings for Global/1st person/3rd person into it’s own subsections as follows:
			GLOBAL
				Smoothing
				Invert X-axis (bring in from Mouse section)
				Invert Y-axis (bring in from Mouse section)
			1st person:
				Field of View slider
				Camera Bobbing toggle
				Rotation Speed Slider (0 – 100 are the values)
			3rd person:
				Rotation Speed Slider (0 – 100 are the values)

	KEYBINDINGS
		Would like to work on this a bit more with you and Dan. This is confusing to me currently with the way it’s broken out both up top and in the subsections within the keybinds and feels like a lot to take in for a player and find what they’re looking for


ADD-ONS
	The top header/line treatment should follow how we have this in social windows.
	x	The horizontal line should be underneath the ‘Add-Ons’ label.
		x	The dropdown should be beneath this horizontal line.
		x	The dropdown should have a label above it similar to what we do elsewhere (label should just say ‘View For:’)
	The column headers:
	x	When sorting, we’ll just want to make sure we use the same art treatment as we do in other columns that sort.
		x	i.e. arrows next to the labels. For the on/off column it’s two arrows above/below each other.
	x	For the name, have that line up with the column label for ‘Name’. Have the +/- be outside.
	x	+/- will need to use the triangle art we use elsewhere to indicate expanded/collapsed states

	System Messages
	-	provide an area for system messages (see WoW for example) than can be used to show dependencies, out of date status, error messages
	x	Provide button/switch that lets you "Load Out of Date" Add-Ons

	Character Management
	-	Allow character management for in-game Add-Ons panel

CREDITS
-	I know this is still in flux with typography and layout. We’ll want to hide the Game Menu while credits is going on and treat this as a separate ‘view’ similar to what we do for the movie that pops up (where Alt is just the key that’s present to exit).

2013-11-12 13:41:19
I just took a detailed look at the current state of the console build. Its not as far along as I had ben led to believe and there's plenty of room for strategy, direction, exploration and design. Some preliminary notes:

OVERALL IMPRESSIONS
The design is scaled for sitting 1 foot away from the monitor (as if it was on a PC). I was sitting about 3 feet away from the screen
- had a hard time connecting visual elements
	-	nameplates/healthbars
	-	hint bar
	-	controller icons in panels
-	had a hard time reading text
-	not seeing strong pathways for my eye to follow, instead my eyes (and hands) are unsure of themselves
	-	by comparison, one of the strongest features of the PC UI is the consistency of placement for all elements. This has naturally been reinforced by experience, but I recall it being one of my initial comments to Chris during my interview
-	My gut feeling is that we need to show less at one time. Due to usage context and viewing distance, a TV and computer monitor of equivalent size and resolution are different animals (provide diagram to illustrate point of sharp display all over vs eyescanning a point of interest with everything else blurred out)


IN-WORLD
-	The compass works and feels better using a game controller than it does using a mouse on the PC
-	Player nameplates feel wrong. may be due to positioning & size, but is also related to typography isses


INVENTORY
-	I'm not sure this approach to inventory will work as designed. Given that inventory management for players, vendors and loot is such a key part of the game, I feel that it would be worthwhile to explore other approaches.
	-	Should probably use a different paradigm than is used on the PC build
		-	Tooltips feel extremely detached from item listing
		-	Unclear how item comparison will be managed
	-	I generally like how equipped items are arranged about the character model
		-	See CONTROL SCHEME notes
			-	Unclear how to switch between viewing inventory & viewing equipped geart

ABILITIES
-	Interesting approach to Abilities that feels less generalized than the PC build
	-	Is not clear that the linear approach being taken here fully matches the system model of progression. Did not spend enough time with it to examine further
	-	Overall feels "twitchy", and not in a positive sense
	-	Ability purchase presents a second view of Abilities that feels disconnected from Ability Preview

CONTROL SCHEME
-	As a first time user, the control scheme defies expectations I've become used to
	-	Back/EXIT is implemented inconsistently. It should, in all cases, be a single button, most likely (B)
-	Scrolling with the analog stick is useful, but need to provide redundant method of scrollong with the DPAD in all cases
-	There are a few cases such as skills & inventory where sub-menuing is being introduced to expose top-level categories
	-	filtering an inventory list using a submenu increases perceived complexity and removes players from the the reasons they've opened the panel in the first place
	-	In these cases, I feel it is more useful to use a "tabbed" behavior instead, where LB/RB are used to move between tabs (common to many games)

GAME MENU
-	I would prefer not to design the top-level navigation system as a circle
	-	Adding and removing items from the menu becomes problematic, as the circular form implies symmetry
	-	Radial menus are more effectively used "on the fly" to maintain immersion
			-	Features such as using quickslots or selecting abilities will work well in radial form
				-	In these cases, using a"twitchy" control is a benefit
	-	I feel like the radial form is an indirect way to accomplish entry into high level system features
		-	Relies too heavily on icons
			-	Would probably become second nature after repeated use
	-	I did not feel like I was in contol of the menu, I had to make an effort to use it
		-	I needed to use 2 hands to operate the menu
			-	While I'm not against using 2 hands to manipulate the interface, I feel the technique is misplaced in this context
	-	As a right handed person, the radial menu navigation scheme felt like it was designed for someone left-handed
		-	I feel that the design shouldn't reflect "handedness" at all


HINT BAR
	-	Items on the hint bar are clustered as though their positioning was relevant to their usage
		- For console UI, ordering is relevant to usage (i.e. the cancel/back/negative choice is consistently ordered on the left or right) however position is irrelevant - those choices are all equally available
			-	When looked at from a distance, I felt like my available options were spread apart and I had to work to find them
			-	Would prefer to see these options clustered together, so they can be parsed as a single  group
		-	By comparison, On the PC build, the position of an item on the hint bar actually is relevant to its usage. Although keybinds are widely available, when the mouse is used, it must physically travel to the target. We exploit consistent positioning to build muscle memory

VISUAL DESIGN
	-	A number of stylistic elements have been introduced into this design that have no equivalent in the existing visual language. In some cases, I think these look interesting, in other cases, not so interesting
	-	TOOLTIPS
		-	The ornamentation that has been placed around tooltips adds an interesting flavor, however, I feel it is generically "Medieval" and does not reference the rich history/lore of the world.
		-	The ornamentation used here feels "feminine", when I'd prefer it wasn't gendered at all, or was relevant to racial elements in the existing lore

	-	DIAGONAL FRAMING OF CONTROL PANELS
		-	There's a framing device being used for panels that introduces a strong diagonal element
			-	Feels very out of place - verging on a science fiction idiom
			-	I dont feel we should go there
				-	I understand why this device is being used to encapsulate text labels and controls
				-	I feel that other possibilities can be explored

	-	TYPOGRAPHY
		- 	lacks visual presence and hierarchy
		-	increases perceived complexity
		-	Strongly suggest legibility testing to determine appropriate parameters for a body font, and go no smaller than that

CHAT & FRIENDS
	-	As a social game, these features are the core upon which everything else is based, otherwise this is a single player game with social features tacked on.
	-	Unclear at this time how we will accomodate & include OS level friend & chat features


TODO: PROPOSE STRATEGIC SOLUTIONS FOR THE PROBLEMS YOU IDENTIFIED

2013-11-18 22:06:38
revisiting addons and summarizing whats being implemented and what I've learned about them

IS THE ADDON INSTALLED CORRECTLY?
If an addon appears in the addon list then it means the addon has been installed correctly
When a user enables an addon he is saying "I would like to load this addon"
	- this does not mean the addon will be loaded

IS THE ADDON REALLY ENABLED
An addon must be enabled for it to be loaded into the game
If the switch next to an addon is "ON" then the addon is enabled for loading
	-	the game will attempt to load this addon
If the switch next to an addon is "OFF" then the addon is disabled for loading
	-	the game will not attempt to load this addon

If an addon is shown in red there is a problem
	-	there may be a dependency
		-	the game will be unable to load this addon until the dependency is resolved
			-	when the dependency is resolved is the addon enabled or disabled?
				-	I took the time to resolve the dependency, so I expect that it will be enabled. I can always choose to disable it if thats not what I want
	-	the addon is out of date
		-	the game will be unable to load this addon unless the "load out of date" addons checkbox is selected
			-	when I allow out of date addons, are those addons enabled or disabled?
				-	Those addons may not actually load despite allowing them
				-	There may be many out of date addons in the list
				-	Some of those out of date addons may have dependencies
				-	It is safest if out of date addons default to disabled when I allow them. I can activate them individually if I choose to

busy morning making my will tangible by reviewing updated addons manager design with Andy & Chris and speaking with jared about consolue UI development. Informal interviews with content QA about what they'd like to see in the compass. Lots of feedback, some of which is immediately actionable

COMPASS FEEDBACK

"I lose track of where my friends and foes are when in boss battles"

"sometimes I lose my bearings and I dont know where I am"

In general people liked how quests work - points of interests, breadcrumbing

"Quests you can pick up and next steps look too similar"
(do they?)
"Door pins dont read well. shape is hard to distinguish"
(basically, its a door shape, but maybe some detail on the edges like little bricks?"


Chris mentioned breadcrumbing for when group members are in different areas

"wish I could see more stuff"
"if I could see more I'd feel more like I was in the world"
"doesnt communicate enough to the player"
"I don't always notice when a point of interest is above or below me. I wish the icon could tell me this"


"Its hard for me to tell how far away points of interest really are"
"more situational awareness when I approach things"
"could we add numeric distances to points of interest that I'm facing?"
"I never know where I am because as soon as I approach a location, its name is removed from the compass"
	- can the name of an area persist seperately from the compass?

several people mentioned that they wish they could set custom waypoints
	- another guy wanted to share waypoints with his guild so they could be directed to specific locations
several people mentioned that they wanted to filter icons on the compass to show as much or as little detail as they wanted






ACTIONABLE DESIGN ITEMS
-	I'm unable to see friends or foes when behind me
	-	one approach to solving this is to have the compass behave as though there is a horizontal split through the middle.
		-	friends and foes in front of me appear on the top
		-	friends and foes behind me appear on the bottom
	- friends and foe icons will need to be changed to a form that hugs the edge of the compass (triangular)

-	Its not obvious where my friends or group members are
	-	do they not show up on the compass by design?
		-	if so, why?

-	place greater emphasis on "North"
	-	The intent here is to provide a visual anchor that helps orient players
		-	this is a srtandard that appears on military HUD's
	-	de-emphasize E,W, & S by using smaller font?
	-	add additional gfx to north that overlaps compass edge so that particular direction is clearly visible




-	alter the sizes/alpha/coefficients of compass icons so that distance-driven effects are more exaggerated
	-	Not seeing a problem here. They seem well tuned

-	the glows around new quest icons (what are these technically called  - Quest Offers?) are misleading when viewed side by side with the quest icon I'm currently tracking


- when locations are behind me, I see them behave erraticaly on the compass, any slight movement sends them sliding around then they disappear. can we just not show locations if they're outside ny field of view?

2013-11-20 11:44:42
chatted w Chris at length about the issues he wants to fix with the compass
they come down to making state changes of quest iconography more obvious:
Focused/Not Focused
Current/Available
Discovered/Not Discovered
Glowing/Not Glowing

Define these states further before proceeding.

What does FOCUSED mean?
	I can accept multiple quests and may be working through several of then at a given time, however only one of those is tracked by the quest tracker UI. I am able to cycle through the questslines I've already accepted. The one shown in the Quest Tracker is the focused one

what does AVAILABLE mean?
	Quests can be picked up or continued anywhere. Any given area may be populated with:
		Quest objectives for my FOCUSED quest
		Quest objectives for a quest I accepted but that isnt currently FOCUSED
		Quest givers for new quests
	Each of these cases describe AVAILABLE quests

what does DISCOVERED mean?
	UNDISCOVERED locations show up on the compass when they're nearby (colored black)
	A location is DISCOVERED when you actually visit the place (colored white)

A quest can be either NEW or CURRENT
	An CURRENT quest may be ACTIVE or FOCUSED

A location can either be NEW or DISCOVEREDnew

Does NEW == NEW? yes
Does DISCOVERED == ACTIVE? yes

2013-12-04 10:14:06
working through provisioning system to verify that it can work with the animation conceit I'm inventing. I've encapsulated my basic idea for making crafting feel better in a simple phrase - "Think of it as a cash register. It keeps a running log of the things you do and earn"

This solution adresses user needs:
-	as a player I want to review the progress I'm making
	-	how many potions did I create?
	-	how many enchantments did I create?
	-	what have I learned?
	-	how much have I been inspired?

This solution addresses layout needs
-	strongly couples messaging layout with the flow of crafting
-	eliminates need for modal dialogs when new things are learned

2014-01-07 14:58:04
reading through spellcrafting doc. its a new crafting system for post launch

-	The rubbing game
	-	I would like the act of making and erasing marks in my spellbook to relate in some way to the focus or other parameters of the spell I'm making
	-	the physical act of making a rubbing and seeeing an image appear is inherently engaging. I want to do the rubbing myself rather than watching my character do it
	-	As an artist, I'm as interested in erasing as I am in making a mark.
	-	By including an erase capacity in the "rubbing game", I am able to make choices while physically interacting with the world. Some kind of feedback would need to be occurring to help guide my activities

2014-01-20 11:53:24
Good advice provided during console UI scrum about the UI audit:
"Go deep instead of broad"
-	focus on a single screen to start with and point out:
	-	design concerns
	-	red flags
	-	what's working
	-	what's not working
	-	opportunities

(My own advice)
-	then do the same for another screen
-	give yourself the space to have an opinion
	-	don't react to what the rest of the team, the studio, etc. are doing
		-	doing so puts you in a reactive position and your ideas and opinions will only matter in opposition to someone elses

	-	Set layout constraints
	-	Establish initial interaction design patterns using selected screens as examples
		-	Character Select
		-	Character Create
		-	Inventory
		-	Stats
		-	Skills
		-	Player Menu
		-	Global
			-	Modal dialogs
			-	Hint bar
			-	Item Tooltips
			-	Ability Tooltips

		-	Itemized list of UI systems and components
	-	Presentation

-	I will discuss the high level issues uncovered in my UI research
	-	typography
		-	What is the minimum desirable font size
		-	Can Univers 59 UltraCondensed be used
		-	What is the default font size (body text)
		-	How is font hierarchy being defined?
	-	visual design
		-	I want to use the PC product as a baseline for visual design of the console title
		-	I want to rethink layout for item tooltips
		-	I want to rethink layout for ability tooltips
	-	interaction design
		-	I want to avoid using controller icons in layouts
		-	I want to use the hint bar to consolidate iconic representations of available actions
		-	I want the player menu to be horizontal not radial
			-	I want the player menu to follow the same "last used available when invoked" pattern used on the PC project
		-	I want the game menu to be a vertical structure placed at the side of the screen
		-	I want to discuss how to invoke the player menu and game menu using PS4 game controller

2014-02-04 10:12:41
working from home today
setup syncbackSE profiles to automate get and push file operations
played with player menu doc and revised it
established a name for a navigation pattern:

NESTED MULTISELECTION

the CURRENT ITEM can lead to a SCREEN or to another ITEM
the CURRENT ITEM can be OPENED/FOLLOWED by pressing CONFIRM or SCROLLING+
the CURRENT ITEM always has a NEXT ITEM
the CURRENT ITEM always has a PREVIOUS ITEM
the PREVIOUS item can lead to another ITEM or to the TOP MENU
the NEXT ITEM can lead to another ITEM or a SCREEN
when an ITEM leads to itself it ends the chain in that direction

summarized:
items can be arranged in chains
the items at the beginning and end of a chain are its endpoints
one of the items in the chain is always the current item
the current item always leads to another item, screen or menu.
the current item defines a previous item
the current item defines a next item
the current item can be followed by pressing confirm or scrolling+/-

2014-02-05 20:55:01
I came home and looked over my work and had a crisis feeling - the design intention is sound. The mechanic for switching contexts - between "equipment" and "supplies" felt too loose and was raising questions for me. I'd sketched other variants of this layoiut, and it seems now that mapping the contexts: equipment/supplies to L1/R1 is the simple solution. It also opens up fluid navigation to get into and out of the inventory list from the category list - no confirmation step required to open a "slot"  - fallback allows confirm button to perform redundant nav as scroll

2014-02-05 23:24:05
my revision just got real. The context switching I'd previously been doing - using fluid navigation to change contexts  between equipment and supplies didn't match a self-evident controller mapping. Solid design

2014-02-20 16:52:04
QA feedback from Bethesda (Rockville)
Least liked features of controls
-	Controls for dodge and sneak are universally disliked as too cumbersome to use
-	Current button mapping for abilities feels awkward.
	-	Using X and B on XB1 (Square and Circle on PS4) for abilities is not a natural finger placement as they are on opposite sides. X/Y or Y/B (Square/Triangle or Triangle/Circle on PS4) make more sense
	-	Testers frequently pressed the wrong button for abilities
	-	Testers would prefer A/Cross acts as jump, X/Square act as interact (current scheme is Y/Triangle for jump, A/Cross for interact)
-	Most menus felt too buried, requiring lots of time to do simple tasks
	-	Leveling up and slotting abilities can take 8+ actions to do
	-	Frustrating in a game where pausing is not an option

2014-02-22 21:46:42
Ah crap, the navigation is more complex than I recognized. It has 3 levels, not 2
In keeping with simplifying the controls used to navigate, it may be more reasonable to use a 2 column layout with no L2/R2 catagory navigation
In this scenario, you'd scroll through Nav2 vertically, and access the nav3 contents horizontally. nav1 would be relegated to some kind of column header for Nav2. Not directly interactable.
What's wrong with that idea?
-	In order to access Craft skills from Class skills, you'd have to scroll vertically through something like 3 + 6 + 3 + 1 + 2 + 2 + 1 items. That's 18 items! That's too much.

The better idea is to allow navigating thru categories as previously expected using L2/R2, however the navigation is presented in a 2 column grid


<	CLASS	>
> Aedric Spear		Ultimate Ability
Dawn's Wrath		Passive Abilities <...>
Restoring light		Passive Abilities <...>

2014-02-27 10:53:02
defining sprint tasks
Release 14 Sprint 2
sprint ends: 3/21/2014

As a designer I want all wireframe layouts to be visually consistent and easily editable
-	Revise all wireframes to use full screen width and consistent metrics for font and layout
5h

As a player I want to see how buttons are mapped to the controller
-	create controller diagram incorporating current mapping scheme
5h

As a developer I want to choose a predefined character template when I create a new character
-	revise character creation design to include character tenmplate selection
3h

As a player I want to trade items with others
-	revise inventory design to include scenario for trading items
12h

As a player I want to place items in the bank
-	revise inventory design to include scenario for banking
8h

As a player I want to pick up loot in the world
-	revise inventory design to include scenario for loot pickup
8h

As a designer I want a unified hint bar
-	create hint bar design that incorporates the following:
	-	unified approach to button usage for primary actions, secondary actions, options, and chat
	-	social notifiers (messages, mail, notifications)
8h

As a player I want to chat when I'm in front end screens
-	create wireframes for text & voice chat UI
20h

As a designer I need to know what the UI looks like
-	visual design for Inventory
-	visual design for Skills
-	visual design for Character Creation
-	visual design for Player Menu
estimate: unknown

As an external developer I want to see documentation that communicates my client's vision for the UI
-	collate UI design audit
-	review UI design audit w ZOS
-	review UI design audit with IG
20h

Task total 89h

2014-04-08 15:55:20
interesting but somewhat frustrating discussion wLee & Kurt about Paul's stated requirements for voice/text chat. Perhaps I've been thinking about it backwards, with text chat being the primary use case, rather than voice?

Reiterating Paul's requirements to make sure I've understood them correctly:

REQUIREMENTS
-	Voice chat isn't invoked or triggered, all players have an open mic
-	Chat windows filter the textstream to show relevant information for the current VOIP channel
-	Players are always in the /say channel
	-	The /say channel can be muted
	-	Players can speak in the /say channel at any time (at all times?)
		-	/say can be heard at a radius around the speaking player

-	Group chat is available whenever a player joins a group
	-	group members can speak at any time and hear others in their group
	-	group members are automatically members of their corresponding group channel
	-	group members automatically leave a group channel when they leave the group

-	Guild channels become available when a guild is formed
	-	Guild members are automatically members of their guild channel
	-	Guild members can speak and listen by by default
		-	The guild admin can set speaking permissions for individuals
		-	The guild admin can set listening permissions for individuals
		-	The guild admin can set speaking permissions by rank
		-	The guild admin can set listening permissions by rank
	-	Guild channels are mutually exclusive
		-	Players can speak and hear one guild channel at a time

-	Players can browse and inspect a running log of people speaking in the current channel
-	Players can browse and inspect the textstream for the current channel
	-	This log enables players to take action on those listed
		-	gamercard
		-	options
			-	mute
			- 	call to party
			-	block
			-	whisper
			-	travel to player
		-	view item in chat


DEFINITIONS
-	What does speaking mean?
	-	Is it a continuous feed from an open mic that registers as input above particular threshold?
	-	Does the player open the mic (by holding a button on the game controller?)

-	What is a channel?
	-	a channel is a VOIP channel in which a player has the ability to speak and listen
		-	guild channel permissions (speak/hear) can be set for individuals by the guild leader
		-	guild channel permissions (speak/hear) can be set for memmber ranks by the guild leader
		-	channels can be muted entirely by the player

-	What is the textstream?
	-	the textstream is the ongoing stream of timestamped text generated by player data entry or NPC's

-	What is a chat window?
	-	A chat window is a filtered view of the textstream
	-	New chat windows can be created and named by players to provide easy access to different filter configurations


QUESTIONS
-	How do I speak?
	-	anything you say is registered to the CURRENT channel
-	How do I know who is speaking?
	-	username + speaking = true is timestamped/registered in chat stream
-	How is speaking represented visually?
-	How many channels can I speak in at once?
	-	one
-	How many channels can I listen to at once?
	-	two
		-	CURRENT channel
		-	/SAY
			-	say can be muted
-	Why would I want to create a custom chat window?
	-	indeed
		-	 the group & guild channels behave as custom windows in thatthey filter the chat stream

2014-04-10 13:17:42
Reviewing Kurt's chat design

KURTS QUESTIONS:
-	how to signify that I can hear conversations in General when I'm speaking in another channel
	-	use microphone and speaker icons
-	shortcuts for both Whisper and Mute when highlighting players
	-	yes. both
		-	use R3 to mute focused item - whether that is a channel or a person
		-	R3 is used elsewhere as a "discard", and "mark as jumk" action
-	What do we call /say and /zone
	-	I'm OK with calling /say something like "Nearby" or simply "General"
	-	Not seeing a problem with /zone
		-	elaborate further?

DESIGN notes
-	I want to know what channels are available at all times
	-	when I join a channel, I'd like for the other avaulable channels to remain visible, but in an inactive state


SCENARIOS COVERED BY THE DESIGN
-	Joining an existing channel
-	Typing a response to a player who may not have a mic
-	Muting/Unmuting a channel
-	Leaving a channel
-	Selecting players from chatstream (Travel to Player)
-	Muting another player
-	Returning to general chat from another channel
-	You join the text-only zone channel while simultaneously speaking in the General channel
-	Tell. Your group is disbanded and you ask a previous groupmate to invite you to a new group
-	You accept a group invite and are automatically placed into the group channel
-	View linked item


SCENARIOS
-	I just joined the game, I'm not in a group or guild
	-	what do I see when I open the chat panel

-	I was listening and speaking in the General channel then I joined a group
	-	what messaging do I see in the HUD that lets me know I'm now speaking in the group channel?
	-	what do I see in the chat panel to indicate that I'm still listening to the GENERAL channel?

CONCERNS
-	confusion betweem closing the chat panel and leaving a channel
	-	Lets use the DPAD to toggle the chat panel open/closed
	-	Lets use O/B to leave a channel
		-	If you back out to the point where you are no longer in any channels, the O/B will exit the chat panel

-	when selecting a player from the chatstream, I want to view their gamercard by default

-	I'd like for R3 to be used as mute/unmute in all cases
	-	If I'm focused on a channel, it mutes/unmutes the channel
	-	If I'm focused on a player, it mutes/unmutes the player

-	did not fully understand the scenario "Returning to General Chat"
	-	it appeared that I was able to scroll between different VOIP channels without exiting the channel I was already in?
	-	I want to minimize the possible state of the channel list
		-	Whenever I step out of a chennel (for example to unmute GENERAL), I effectively leave that channel
		-	In the scenario provided, I'd expect the following to happen:
			-	I leave the group channel
			-	I scroll to the General channel
			-	I unmute the General channel
			-	I scroll back to the group channel
			-	I rejoin the group channel

-	the zone channel
	-	What if /Zone was a subset of GENERAL
	-	When I am in the GENERAL channel:
		-	I can switch between ALL/NEARBY/ZONE using L2/R2
		-	If I speak, I am always heard by the people nearby me - regardless of whether I'm viewing ALL/NEARBY/ZONE
		-	The text stream is filtered appropriately depending on whether I'm in ALL/NEABY/ZONE

2014-04-15 14:49:24
came across this gem. Its innacurate, but was my entry point into the navigational scheme I'm designing. Would be great to critique and correct this to my current state of knowing

NESTED MULTISELECTION
(this is what I was calling fluid navigation at tthe time)

the CURRENT ITEM can lead to a SCREEN or to another ITEM
the CURRENT ITEM can be OPENED/FOLLOWED by pressing CONFIRM or SCROLLING+
the CURRENT ITEM always has a NEXT ITEM
the CURRENT ITEM always has a PREVIOUS ITEM
the PREVIOUS item can lead to another ITEM or to the TOP MENU
the NEXT ITEM can lead to another ITEM or a SCREEN
when an ITEM leads to itself it ends the chain in that direction

summarized:
items can be arranged in chains
the items at the beginning and end of a chain are its endpoints
one of the items in the chain is always the current item
the current item always leads to another item, screen or menu.
the current item defines a previous item
the current item defines a next item
the current item can be followed by pressing confirm or scrolling+/-

2014-05-27 13:53:15
what are common user stories for vendor inventory? referencing the game to find out

As a player:
-	I want to buy an item
-	I want to know when I can afford an item
-	I want to sell an item from my inventory
-	I need to know my inventory capacity
-	I need to know how much gold I have
-	I want to buy back an item I sold
-	I want to repair an item
-	I want to repair all my items
-	I want to know the name of the vendor I'm interacting with
-	I want to see items in categorized views identical to those used in my player inventory
-	When selling items I want to have the option to equip, use or destroy items already in my inventory
-	I want to compare vendor equipment with my currently equipped gear
-	When buying consumables I want to be able to purchase more than one at a time
-	I want to know how many instances of a stackable item I already have

2014-06-12 23:27:20
RFeviewed Jason's animation work and edited in AE to speed up the transitions. I think that its coming along well, but the one part I'm missing is a strong visual cue directing me to "open the box" - that is - dive into categories and so forth.
For one thing, its not obvious when I can stop navigating. How many levels does the menu have?

I think that by stacking the panels before they become visible, the menu depth can be conveyed. Once there's no more stacked paneling, there's nowhere else to go forward.

As far as the visual cue to dive in, there needs to be some kind of attachment to the focal point. Not sure what that would be now, especially since the menus are behaving like accordions at the top level...

2014-06-12 23:32:30
On the inventory screen - what happens to the NAV2 panel when moving from an equipment slot (such as Main Hand) to Supplies?
-	does the panel just go away (cut away?) when Supplies is focused, and then return when focus switches to Main Hand (again - simply a cut?)
-	What is shown in the NAV2 panel when an equipment slot is empty?

2014-06-13 08:42:20
having new thoughts about inventory comparison. There's too much indeterminacy when "opening" an inventory category. A simp,ified version goes like this:
-	Open Player Menu and choose Inventory
-	Open Inventory Screen NAV1 and observe which slots are occupied
-	Scroll to occupied slot and open it to view inventory items and present them in NAV2
-	Comparison is not implicit, but must be invoked by pressing a button on controller
	- does comparison stay active at this point or is it modal?
	- doe I need to exit comparison mode or is it a toggle?

2014-06-13 15:05:50
working through use cases for skill assignment/rearranging

2014-06-15 17:06:32
sketched my way through a number of schems for skill assignment and rearranging. moving those ideas into fireworks

2014-06-16 23:40:31
Good consultations w Jason, Dan, Alex and Marc have led to a better understanding of assinging & placing skills. I've got 2 schemes in mind.
The first is the evolution of the original idea, highly optimized workflow, and pretty clever and I think plausible as well. The focus here is on slots - what goes in them, how to move skills between them,, and so forth

Another approach that suggests itself is to treat this screen like the PC keybinding UI. Its about controller configuration, not slots. I associate skills with the game controller - what button is it assigned to, what button to I want to assign it to. In this scheme, you can scroll to view skill details, but assignment and place ment is done using the existing HUD controller mapping. This approach is in my view actually far more straightforward than the liust based approach and I suspect it will be the preferred treatment.

LIST FOCUS RULES

If the slot is populated then initial focus of the equipment list is on the equipped item

If the slot is unpopulated then initial focus of the equipment list is on first item in the equipment list

If the slot is empty then the equipmenbt list empty state is shown

2014-07-03 00:32:29
just sketched out an item-centered approach to inventory - based in part on some feedback from Matt P, one of the QA leads. This approach significantly reduces the need to dive into and out of categories, and finally provides a view all list in addition to categorized views such as weapons, armor, consumables, etc. Where this approach really shines though is that item equipping/unequipping is done with R1 for general purpose and the right hand, and with L1 for the left hand.

2014-07-07 03:30:42
I've fleshed out my new vison for an item based inventory. I believe the design is easier to use, and in fact could be even easier were we not using LT/RT as a general purpose method for jumping between list categories. The most natural mapping for equiupping would be as Skyrim does, where equipping items is related to the hand they go in. At the end of the day (literally) its not a huge issue. the only time equipping is modal is when something can be dual wielded. In all other cases, simply pressing CONFIRM equips the item

2014-07-07 20:03:20
reviewing my inventory design

2014-07-08 13:34:51
overall details of inventory design were well recieved or invisible. Some push back from the team though to maintain the slot-based approach as implemented. Which is actually possible...
I'm mocking that up now and seeing where it falters.

2014-07-08 17:29:32
I mocked up a hybrid slot-centric approach which includes New, All, Quickslot, Consumable & Supply categories. It seems OK, I'm not convinced that equipping items from a slot is the best UX, but mechanically its been shown that slot-centric and category-centric approaches can both work, and evern co-exist. Doing some final cleanuop and handing off to Dan.

2014-07-11 11:21:11
excellent discussion w Nick about my generalized design approach to quickslots. He suggested a more modal approach that I feel would also work, but that comes with the cost of always diving in and out of content. I'm moving forward to fleshing out my initial idea in FW to see how it presents visually. I'll also moch up the modal version we discussed and will evaluate further from there.

2014-07-11 11:30:40
I extended the work we did on skills,. I like how that system works, but I dislike that my initial slot assignment takes me to a seperate slot assignment page where I can select skills and slots independently, whereas my subsequent slot assignments keep me on that page (I don't have to go anywhere)

I don't feel that my initial selection should be privileged this way. Instead of ordering content in time, it can be ordered in space. In my design approach

The most immediate way to deal with this (for assign from slot) is to have my EDIT ACTIONBAR CTA take me immediately to a state where I can selet slots and skills independently (thus removing the modality)

2014-07-11 13:37:44
The interaction patterns for slot assign are complex enough that I cant easily distinguish the relative merits of the 2 different approaches on paper. I'm going to create simple Axure prototype (w xpadder driving keyboard input) to see how these patterns feel. In the meantime I can finalize Character Selection and update that documentation.

2014-07-13 02:24:30
After additional thinking on slot assign, the modal approach is the way to go. The only question ius whether it is entirely list based or affordance based. Either case would work, but the list based approach allows vertical navigation to be used in all cases, whereas the affordance based approach gives you a representation to work with. Management is possible in both cases, but must be invoked as an option for assign from slot

2014-07-31 22:44:41
There is an alternate inventory design, one in which equipment slots are more closely coupled to the player, or are at least overlaid on top of the environment. You can view those slots as empty slots or as slots filled with an icon. Only when you use the right stick do those slots come to life, showing item name and tooltip  - layout of that TBD. The increased control requires increased complexity in the interaction design - perhaps not overly so though. Basically, while navigating through slots with the right stick, you'd need to make a selection while holding the stick, and pressing an easily accessible button like RT. Which actually feels pretty good when I try this w game controller. Another requirement here is that the equipment slots are only activated while the stick is being held. When the stick is in a rest state, those slots revert to their defauly (inactive) state.

How to test this idea? Ask Jason to set up a quick prototype where moving the right stick causes an inactive target to "become active/focused", and releasing it causes the target to deactivate.

A further test would be to cause multiple arbitraryily placed targets to app(ear (clustered so they're obvs together). Moving the stick causes focus to shift to each of them incrementally. Releasing the stick causes them to deactivate.

2014-08-05 23:16:56
quickslot assignment really is more straightforward when you must push the stick in the direction of the slot you want to assign to and then press confirm

here's how it works
-	the player can exit the quickslot UI at any time, whether an assignment has been made or not
-	assigning an item to a quickslot from an inventory list places it into a pocket (a temporary storage area) at the center of the quickslot wheel
-	the attached tooltip panel always shows what the joystick is focused on
-	when the stick is at rest, focus is on the pocket in the center of the wheel
-	pushing the stick focuses on the slots in the quickslot wheel
-	focusing on a quickslot and CONFIRMING can have the following outcomes
	-	if nothing was in the pocket, the item in the quickslot is transferred to the pocket
	-	if nothing was in the quickslot, the item in the pocket is transferred to the quickslot
	-	if nothing was in either the pocket or the quickslot, then nothing happens
	-	if both the pocket and the quickslot are occupied then these items are swapped

2014-08-05 23:49:55
-	I've been hesitating on posting the quickslot design to confluence because I wasnt satisfied with my initial lowest commion denominator solution. The one mapped out above is the better choice, but to convey my intent - eachj of the use cases mentioned there needs to be diagrammed

2014-08-07 12:40:25
initial alchemy implementation is in. its hideous, but it works!

feedback

-	show item counts attached to item icons, not the item labels
-	replace workbench area with neutral visual design matching wireframes
-	the calls to action on the hint bar are spaced poorly and there are too many of them
	-	show only these icons
		-	Add
		-	Remove
		-	Clear Selections
		-	Craft
-	Assigned items are reading poorly. Instead of dimming the selected item, place a checkmark to the left of it (similar to equipped item in inventory)
-	Focused item & traits list spacing is too tight. Need to refine that layout
-	When moving between reagents and solvents the list "bounces" and should not
-	Need to see visual for unpopulated trait slots in the list
-	Preview tooltip needs to be designed & laid-out to match console standard
-	Panel header needs to be laid out to match the header treatent seen in inventory screen
-	All messaging should be normalized to take place in the upper right - the "greek chorus" area

2014-08-19 17:40:30
just Chatted w Chip & Dan (the new Dan) about the Player XP meter (same component used for Alchemy, Smithing Etc.)
They'd brought up some technical concerns assocated with character stats where the meter shiftw oosition horizontally. Thing is that meter has animations that auto trigger whenever an update is registered.
I suggested that the meter will have a fixed positiin in the lower right instead (similar to its fixed position in the upper left on the PC) . Its a solid call, and definitely gives me confidence that this is the appropriate presentation. The engineers seemed satisfied with it as well.

2014-08-22 10:11:22
TALKING POINTS

-	What were the goals of the Sprint?
	-	GOALS
		-	Communicate the design to Zindagi and establish iterative working methods
		-	Apply visual and behavioral standards to UI templates for consitency
		-	Refine Player Inventory design to better understand what's working and what isn't

	-	 Accomplished

		-	Continued standardization for

		-	Design and implement crafting systems
			-	(implemented) Alchemy
			-	(implemented) Provisoning
			-	(implemented) Alchemy
			-	(in progress) Smithing

		-	Implement pregame systems
			-	(implemented) Login Flow (first pass)
			-	(implemented) Character Selection

		-	Player Inventory refinements
			-	Added Inventory Categories
				-	Supplies
				-	Materials
				-	Quickslottable
			-	Implemented Add to Quickslot from inventory
			-	Refined Equipment Slots Design to not show unpopulated slots

		-	Numerous designs completed to provide a backlog for engineering which will allows design to stay ahead of implementation and respond to work in progress
			-	Stables
			-	Character Sheet
			-	AVA screens
			-	Contacts
			-	Mail


-	What were impediments that contributed to stories not being delivered
	-	There's a significant overhead associated with working remotely with Zindagi, which becomes especially evident when iterating and refining features
			-	I traveled to their studio to work with them for a week so that I could get hands on with Crafting designs and other low level UI systems, such as list formatting and typography
			-	We've been communicating well, and have gotten better at it,

-	Lessons learned
	-	As more designs are completed, patterns emerge and problems are solved, which informs and improves each new design. We are designing faster and better.

2014-09-18 13:06:14
Functional spec for Map Screen

zoom in/out
pan in any direction (using analog stick)
immediately jump to current location
drop marker at current location (i.e. set destination)

quests
view list of available quests
jump to location of any listed quest

legend
show legend off all map icons
show tooltip for any map marker under the cursor

locations
view maps for all listed locations
show name of current location
show current time

bounties
show bounty for current location

filters
allow map filters (on/off) for objectives, wayshrines & group members

Keep Upgrade window
map screen should support the display of the Keep Upgrade window

We do not need to show character name and level here, replace this informartion with Location Name, Location Bounty, Current Time

We do not need the ability to toggle map size

RECEIVE MONEY (C.O.D.)

choosing send opens a currency spinner in a dropdown

remain cuncertain how to attach items in the simplest way.
The simplest way is to treat each attachment slot like a dedicated slot
You can attach 1 item at a time
Attached items are shown in the equipment list with a checkmark
Choosing a previously attached item unattaches it
Unattaching an item removes it from its corresponding slot
You stay in "attach mode" until exiting w back button

2014-09-26 17:11:18
Rethought item attachment meethod, because it was getting stupidly complex. I have a working solution:

-	Items are always added to the first attachment slot
-	Items already in the attachment list are pushed to the right when a new item is added
-	Only 6 items can be attached at any time
	-	Existing items are pushed to the right when a new item is added
	-	When the list is full and a new item is added existing items are pushed out of the attachment list and are no longer attached
-	When an item is removed from the list all other items are pulled to the left

2014-09-27 09:45:30
I think the major issue I'm having with this attachment flow is a desire to allow player to continue attaching items, which will end up pushing existing attachments off the attachment list. In practice, its not useful. maybe.

2014-09-27 10:40:49

2014-10-07 20:10:55
back in AX
the idea I implemented where paging horizontally moves you through all the skill lines is well-intentioned, and possibly correct, but I got to thinking about how would the pagination control on the L1R1 menu look if all possible skill lines were available?

There would be 24 possible pages at this time, and looking at a ;icture of that layout in AX seems like a pretty dense array of stuff - enough so that its visual clutter even when scaled down. Perhaps it could be made to work.

More from a UX perspective however. Skills are collected in Skill lines for a reason. Everything in a skill line is related, and in practice, not every skill needs to be treated as random access. They're clustered together.

The paging navighation is a good idea - it allows exploration in depth, and perhaps also in a cluster.

-	revert prototype back to scheme where the only paging that happens is within a skill line, rather than across all skill lines (as a comparisoon)

2014-10-07 20:47:26
finished purchase upgrade flow

2014-10-07 22:16:22
I have paging navigation restricted to skill lines, rather than the full skill matrix.

I feel this navigation provides a good overview of a skill line - how the various skills are related and how they are different. Crafting skills for example. When you only have a single skill point to spend, you want to make sure you're spending it wisely - wisdom is gained by knowing the relative advantages and disadvantages the various skill lines. Why should I purt a point intoProvisioniung rather than Enchanting for example? It may be that a passive ability for one of these skill lines  provides a meaningful benefit. Having to dive in and out of various skill line sto gain this information disconnects me from the bigger picture.

2014-10-11 14:39:23
looking at skills again
-	review the actionbar designs jason posted
x	categorize and collate posted design feeback to inform this next iteration
-	review skill usage ingame

FEEDBACK
GAMEPAD REPRESENTATION VS ACTIONBAR REPRESENTATION
-	I find the controller image unnecessary. It is large and dominates the pertinent information: what ability is mapped to what control. I would rather see it however it will ultimately look on the HUD

-	I think the gamepad on the right is a little overkill.  Gamers are smart, they can juggle a total of 6 abilities and their relation to each other on the gamepad in their head at once.  Also the new ActionBar layouts will help keep this clearer too in theory.

-	there is no guarantee X,Y,B,RB,LB and RB+LB are going to be mapped to abilities. if they have to dynamically position themselves based on whatever control schemes we decide to ship with it’s gonna be a nightmare.


I LIKE THAT WE REPRESENT ASSIGNED SKILLS
- 	Does it make sense to indicate if an ability is currently equipped using the language use in other Windows (the check)
-	the display of ability states (highlighting the skill line and individual skills for example) is a nice touch.


ASSIGNMENT UI
-	I also appreciate noting the morph state on the assigned abilities. This shortcuts things for me nicely. Can I act on the morph state attached to the assignment UI? If I see an ability on the right that is noted as being morphable, I would want to quickly locate it In the main menu so I could act on that information.

-	how do I manipulate the assignment UI to move abilities from slot to slot?

SKILL LINE TOOLTIP
-	I do like the skill line and ability tooltip.  I assume this menu will inherit the standard look meaning the tooltip will be smaller and grayer than the main panel so when you dive into it there will be a notable difference inherently.

-	 I like the idea of a skill “tooltip” as you have in the second panel.  It seems like it will fit.  I would recommend making the tooltip have a slightly different look than the panel does when I dive into the skill to spend points (maybe the background color?

-	I feel pretty strongly that the assignment layout should match the action bar HUD layout, or else I’ll need to hold two different representations of it in my head

-	I often switch skills right before a battle, so having my entire screen blocked would be a undesirable hit to my situational awareness.  I think having the second tooltip panel is fine though.

2014-10-11 14:54:18
The scenario I keep returning to involves auto assignment of skills upon purchase. Perhaps I just finished the tutorial and gained a skill point. Upon purchase of the skill it is auto-assigned to the Ability 1 slot. Without visibility of the slot s, I won't know where its been. Yet, the representation of skill we use currently does show this information. The real problem with that to me has always been that I had to scroll to the ability bar representation and select it like a button to put myself in a state where I could assign skills directly. (a double confirmation)

Surely, that system can work more fluidly?

Looking at the vendor inventory design on Confluence, at the previous "light/diminished" panel tratment there looks a lot better to me than using the same panel for everything.


VISUAL DESIGN NOTES
At the very least, the nav2 panel looks best when it is brighter than the nav1 panel

The vertical rails have a rounded/beveled appearance. They should be flat instead

the horizontal rails have a rounded/beveled appearance. They should be flat.

the horizontal rails fade out to quickly. They should not fade out to 0% opacity, and the edges should appear closer to the edge of the panels

The vertical list needs to signify the focused item more explicitly. Need an additional visual cue to show focus

methodically

Creating and Joining Guilds

Guilds are a grat way to team up with other travelers you come across while exploring the world of Tamriel! These large, permanent groups allow players to band together, work towards common goals, and unlock special perks like Guild Stores.

Utilize ranks to make your guild as structured or free-form as you like! Coordinate with your guildmates and fight in large scale battles against others as part of the Aliiance Wars!

Guild membership is tied to your UserID and is limited to 5 guilds total. All characters under your UserID will have access to all of your guilds.

not getting much time to focus on any one thing

Requirement: Important display items are displayed using one of the following methods so they can be
displayed in the display area of a TV.
• Designing the screen so that important display items are included approximately in the center 90% of
the vertical/horizontal measurements (90% x 90% of the area)
• Important display items are included in the safe area, which can be obtained with
sceSystemServiceGetDisplaySafeAreaInfo().
Description
"Important display items" are as follows.
• Error messages and warning messages defined by the TRC
• Legal texts such as the EULA and other copyright indications
• Online IDs used as the identifiers of users
• Operation instructions and game rule explanations
• Other text and graphics required to appropriately advance gameplay
To display long character strings, either provide a sufficient display area for the longest character strings,
or provide a scrolling display. Or, you may provide a function such as "See Details" to display the
character strings on a separate screen.

2014-11-06 11:25:27
reviewing Armor Dyeing 1st implementation

VISUAL
-	Character needs to be positioned to right of window
-	GRB: Color swatches need to communicate empty state more clearly.
	-	When my color presets are empty, is not obvious that there are 3 inndividual swatches in each preset
	-	when my armor color swatches are empty, is not obvious that there are 3 individual swatches to be filled there
-	Radial Menu needs visual cue when in rest state (the focus is at the center of the wheel) that movement is possible along the edges of the wheel when you push the stick

FOCUS
	GRB: When using the radial menu, the focused state of armor and swatches can be communicated more effectively by combining these 2 methods:
		-	the focused item is larger than the non focused items
		-	the specific color swatch that will be activated upon confirm is drawn with a "dim" or otherwise de-emphasized border around it

SEGMENTING FUNCTIONALITY


	I would like to make a strong distinction between interactions on the NAV1 panel (color picker) and interactions on the NAV2 panel (the radial menu). Its interesting that I can randomize and sort the color list at any time, but doing so mixes up UI State (i.e. what am I operating on at the moment) in a way I'm not comfortable with
	-	GRB: The options menu should be unavailable whenever the radial menu is active
	-	GRB: The Randomize functionality should be unavailable whenever the radial menu is active



EXITING/CANCELING
-	Issues backing out of the screen

2014-11-12 11:41:18
ACHIEMENTS feedback

Achievement Icons
-	Icons should be framed to unify their appearance, as is done on the PC
	-	use same framing gfx as used for skills
	-	do not use existing PC-UI art assets for this

-	Un-earned achievement icons are too dim to read

-	When viewing an achievement list I'd like to see the number of achievement points that are rewarded by each achievement (see design doc for visual reference)

-	Include a Summary Page as specified in cleaned up design doc

-	Icons used in the Achievement category list should not be glowing when focused
	-	Am assuming this is because we're using the existing PC icons which have that glow baked in. If this is the case we need a clean variant of these for console usage that omit the glow fx

-	Is not always obvious when the right stick can be used to scroll through lengthy achievement descriptions
	-	include the scroll arrow gfx found in the conversation window (see design doc for visual reference)

-	Long achievement descriptions shoud fade out at the same rate as a parametric list


-	Filtering the list from the options menu feels awkward. This is an issue found on a few other screens, so lets leave this functionality alone until it is determined whether a better solution to list filtering (I have one in mind) is an investment we're prepared to make

-	Layout and typography of achievemeent descriptions should conform to the layout guidelines I've posted to Achievements design doc on confluence


-	Upon diving into a particular Achievement Category (such as Character Achievements) change the title of the panel to reflect the current state. This is a common pattern used elsewhere in the game (see design doc for visual reference)

2014-11-17 12:25:33
data gathering and clustering layout guidelines for upcoming layout guidelines spreadsheet

PANEL HEADER
-	should match visual standards detailed in layout guide
	-	none of this content should ever fall outside of safe frame
	-	should respect panel margins.
		-	Header content should be drawn 40px from the left edge of a panel, and should end no fewer than 40px from the right edge of a panel

PANELS
-	Panel margins are 40px of whitespace on left and right side of the panel
	-	Panel content should generally fit into this area, with the significant exception of the vertical list, which is known to overlap the left margin when utility icons (checkmarks, new indicator, etc.) are attached to list items

-	Panel content should fade out at the same rate
	-	wherever content fades out on the interactive panel should apply to any other panels, unless noted as a special case

TOOLTIPS
-	Tooltips should be placed consistently as specified in layout guide

TYPOGRAPHY
-	text color
-	font size
-	font placement

HINTBAR
-	consistent messaging should be used for actions
	-	the messaging for selecting/confirming items in a list should generally not vary when players scroll through the list, unless screen functionality demands this - (i.e. equip/unequip)
-	minimal number of available actions presented
	-	The Back button should never be shown unless a modal dialog is being shown
	-	The



GFX ASSETS
-	Progress Meters
	-	Meters on this screen use flat console gfx style (rather than swapping in PC graphics)
	-	Meters on the screen should match the meter layout specified in the layout guide
	-	Meter framing used a 2px flat edge without antialiased artifacts

-	Asset Framing
		No framing elemengt should be smaller than 2px. Hairlines should be avoided

	Currency icons
		should use flat console-gfx style
		should be monochromatic. no color coding




VERTICAL LIST
-	focused item matches positioning established in player menu and detailed in layout guide
-	vertical list items have iconography drawn to the immediate left of text label
-

2014-11-17 21:55:33
Map notes
-	quest list is behaving interestingly. Wrongly uses 30/37 px text for list instead of all 30px, as I'd expected. Long focused text labels are wordwrapping. The same unfocused label does not wordwrap. Its not crappy looking, its actually pretty cool, although making this approach win would involve further refinem,ent to the list behavior - remove leading spaces so that newlines don'ty begin with an empty space character.

2014-11-18 21:51:52
ARMOR DYEING v2 NOTES

The wheel element behind the radial menu should only be visible when I'm able to navigate that menu, otherwise it should be hidden

do not use weathered-metal looking frames around color swatches in the radial menu. Instead, use the same treatment for all color swatches on this screen



when the focus of the screen shifts to the radial menu, I feel that the focus of the vertical menu needs to be diminished
	-	I can see now that we change the opacity of the active panel, but because the panel is already black, the darker black is not highly visible
	-	Take a look at what skyrim does - they increase the luminance of the defocused panel


I will specify standardized layout for the vertical list panel

I will specify standardized layout for item tooltip

Item tooltip charge/damage meter doesn't need to be shown when looking at items in this context

Character model needs to be shifted from behind panel to the right side of the screen so color changes are visible while I'm making them

All content in panel 2 needs to be shifted to fall within safe area boundary

Don't show item preview in tooltip area. The tooltip is provided as a convenience, so item names and primary stats are clearly identifiable. Repeating the picture feels redundant. Instead, I'd like to see text that reinforces the actions I can take - such as Dye, Dye All, Copy, etc...

I had asked to show an outline around the the initial color swatch target when an item is focused. Turns out to be confusing, making me feel like I can somehow move the highlight before I've actually activated the color swatch targets


Would the apply color interaction break or feel more precise if when I pressed "back" from within a target color swatch I was kicked back to the vertical list?

The exit dialog that comes up when I back out of the screen is extremely confusing. I always make the wrong choice and get kicked out of the screen
-	should simply state "Apply Changes? Yes/No"

-	Pressing X to apply changes produces an indeterminate result. There's no visible difference between changes that are being previewed and changes that have been applied.
	-	X should be used within the screen to DISCARD CHANGES
	-	Any changes not discarded should be applied upon exiting the screen

When I choose a new mnode such as Dye All and then move up to a preset it switches my mode to Set Fill. If I scroll back down to a color swatch, the mode I previously selected reverts back to Dye All rather than the mode I'd previously chosen

The CLear Color mode, in addition to being non-descoverable, feels like a whole lot of work for nothing. Instead consider assigning a "clear color" function to a face button, which can be used at anyt time I am hovering over a color
	-	What Button though?

using L1/R1 paging nav to switch between modes is non obvious and easily overlooked. The paging metaphor breaks down because the view remains unchanged when I switch modes.
DYE
DYE ALL
CLEAR COLOR
COPY COLOR
