building out functional spec.

DESIGN GOALS

ERRORS
-	show error messages in-place, avoid modal dialogs
-	show last error
-	add prompt for Game Consultant Tool to relevant error messages

SETTINGS
-	make settings prompt more
-	present game and launcher settings in the same place
-	Move Game Consultant prompt

LAYOUT
-	simplify layout to 1 or 2 columns

NEWS FEEDS
- clean up feed text format

ONBOARDING FOR NEW CUSTOMERS
-	include links to guide content on website

MODELESS DIALOGS
-	replace dialogs with panels and use in-place error messages

PROMO
-	Don't box Promo Content in - even if in a panel, it should be borderless
-	bigger hero img

NAVIGATION
-	Remove Store and Patch Notes from navigation
-	Use 3 Tabs to organize the layout better
	-	Game
	-	News
	-	Getting Started

-	Game Actions are independent of current view
	-	Big button showing PLAY/REPAIR/INSTALL always works

GAME MENU
-	Add unique icon/thumbnail for game items
-	Add context menu for game items
	-	Play
	-	Repair
	-	Uninstall
	-	Settings
-	Add double-click actions for game items
	-	depending on build status:
		-	Install
		-	Repair
		-	Play


DRIVE TRAFFIC TO GAME SITE
-	News
-	Guides
-	Platform Services

	Add:
	- Direct Link to landing page (click logo)

FUNCTIONALITY

Provide access to platform services
-	Account
-	Customer Support
-	Forums
-	Realm Status

	Remove:
	-	link to Store
	-	link to patch notes

Dev features
-	Choose build stamp
-	Dev Game Tools
	-	Asset Audit
	-	Market product Crawler
	-	Marketplace Tool
	-	Packfile Manager
-	Multiple builds can be shown in Game Menu
	-	DEV, RC, Coders, etc.

Launcher Settings
-	Megaserver select (North America, Europe)
-	Language select (English, German, French)
-	Run 64 bit client (T/F)
-	Start on Bootup (T/F)
-	Minimize to System Tray (T/F)
-	Show Public Test Environment (T/F)
-	(remove download speed - default to maximum at all times)
-	About the Launcher
-	Game Consultant (button)

Game Settings
-	Auto Update
	-	(x) Always
	-	After Startup
	-	Never
-	Auto Launch (t/f)
-	Create Desktop Shortcut (button)

Game Options (these are settings per build)
-	Check for Updates
-	Repair
-	Install/Uninstall
-	Settings
-	Play

Game Menu Item Types

	-	Play
	-	Repair

-	Messaging

		-	Downloading
		-	Applying Patch
		-	Verifying

	-	Bitrate (MB/s)
	-	Completion %

Game Items

Settings
Play

2016-09-08 13:28:40
think through reviewing promo images w Jared neext week
- what are the benefits?
	-	more sharable
	-	reacquire new users - demonstrate by example that the game has changed
	-	competitive value
		-	this "magazine" style of content presentation is widely found on the web, because it emphasizes story telling within otherwise templated layouts. The form is suitable for day to day deployment, but assumes curation and production workflows in place to support this capability.
		-	our competitors do it
			-	WoW in particular
			-	Destiny
			-	Battlenet
- how can we measure the benefits
???

2016-09-26 11:46:16
Finished updating deliverables spreadsheet and called out mobile design

Hi Steve,

Here is the ESO-PLATFORM UX project status. We'll be going over this work live in the prototype at a stakeholder review later this week. My contract ends this month. We are at a point where we can build this for real. I welcome the opportunity to direct, evaluate and triage the implementation. Can I count on your support?


Solutions
Greater visibility to conversions (Buy Game, Buy Crowns, Join ESO Plus)
Greater visibility to social (feeds, sharing, ESO Live)
Improved Purchase flow (Buy Crowns)
Universal Navigation (integrates BAM, CS, Forums)
Stronger visual identity
Simplicity and scalability for future use (easier to build for)
Increased readability
Workflow Improvements
Began and ended the conversation about "what finished looks like" (it looks like the prototype, screenshots attached)
Introduced live prototyping methods as a design tool for consensus-building and transparency
Worked with IT to setup webserver and version control to support prototyping
Worked with IT, Dev and Production to invent an agile approach to site implementation, with focus on integrating with the new APE CMS tool and live game data as we build
Mobile (These issues also visible on the desktop when browser window is resized)
Mobile designs were previously touched upon, but emerged as a scope-able priority when the design finalized. The redesign accommodates scalability as a core principle, but lacks appropriate layout patterns. Our current support for mobile isn't completely thought through and misses out on certain navigational, readability and commercial goals.

Prototype
http://webdev-uidesign001.z10.zenimaxonline.com/ax_landingpage/landingpage_right-aligned.html
(requires Google Chrome)

2016-10-10 09:55:59
drafting into to Hilary Shapiro

Hi Hilary, I understand that you'll be working with us on the Elder Scrolls Online Platform redesign. Its good to meet you.



I've been working for the past few months with ZOS on UX and Creative for this initiative. Previously, I was the UX Lead at the studio. I helped launch the game on PC. I invented and directed execution of the PS4/XB1 game interface, and brought that over to the PC product by popular demand. You might call me a friend of the game :)



I was asked to work on the ESO-Platform project (game site, account management and the game launcher) because of my

credentials
introduction (agenda)
why am i contacting (outcome)
docs

Hi Hilary, I'm told you'll be taking over ESO-Platform from Steve. I'm an independent devloper working with ZOS on UX and Creative for the game site, account management and the game launcher. Previously I was the UX lead at the studio where I designed and directed game UI for the console and PC products.

Do you have a moment this week to meet up over VOIP? I did a lot of stakeholder research that led to our current approach, so would like to speak to your own concerns and interests. Would also like describe the approach I'm taking a bit further (now with more holistic!).

I've included

Here's a link to the prototype. Requires Chrome. I think you should have access to this, but if not please let me know.

2016-11-14 16:15:03
there are several actions on page load that need to be modified to reach into the menu Master

onPageLoad
set size of mobile menu to ,((Window.height))  Anchor Top Left
move wrapper to ((MENUTOP))

onWindowResize
Set Size of mobile menu to ,((Window.height)) Anchor Top Left

onWindowScroll
Hide mobile menu
Move wrapper to ,((MENUTOP))


OnPageCLick
Hide mobile menu
Move wrapper to ,((MENUTOP))

onAdaptiveViewChange
Hide mobile menu
Set Size of mobile menu to ,((Window.height)) Anchor Top Left

2016-12-06 08:18:18
more clarification on the marketing page
The Buy Now button in the Universal Nav will change to say “Pre-Order Now” and will link to the new “Pre-Order” page. From there, you’ll be able to get to the existing Buy Now page if the user wants to.
·         This will be a de-emphasis of the Buy Now page, which will result is some drop of sales in the name of having many more pre-orders to make up for that.


The Newsletter needs to have a checkbox for being over 18, and should otherwise be reduced to just an email address.
·         This is an increase from 13, the old Legal requirement we had
·         We’re in favor of losing more information about the user (specific age, name, platform and country)
o   We can’t get the country, though we can get the language from the URL

For discussion with William B.

CSS changes

TYPOGRAPHY
.m-box h3 {
	margin: 0;
	font-size: 4vw;
	line-height: 1.7;
	font-weight: 900;
	letter-spacing: 1.2px;
	color:  EECA2A;
	text-transform: uppercase;
	margin-bottom: 0vw;
}

.dlc-newsletter .m-wrap .content-box h3 {
	font-size: 48px;
	line-height: 1.7;
	font-weight: 900;
	letter-spacing: 1.2px;
}

.dlc-news-box .news-snip p {
	height: 50px;
	font-size: 14px;
	line-height: 1.7;
	text-transform: uppercase;
	color:  C5BF9E;
	margin-top: 14px;
	letter-spacing: 0.25px;
}



BUTTON
.m-box .btn-m {
	border: 2px solid  625f4f;
	border-radius: 2px;
	background:  151511;
	font-family: 'Futura PT Cond';
	color:  c5bf9e;
	font-size: 30px;
	text-transform: uppercase;
	height: 70px;
	min-width: 270px;
	padding: 0 20px;
	line-height: 66px;
	vertical-align: middle;
	margin: 0px 5px 30px 5px;
	text-shadow: initial;
	position: relative;
}

LAYOUT/BREAKPOINTS
.m-box .content-box {
	display: block;
	position: absolute;
	color:  FFF;
	margin: 10vw 0 0 0;
	width: calc(100% - 30px)
}



@media (max-width: 1023px) {

	.hero-cinemagraph .m-wrap {
			padding-top: 68px;
			height: 70vw;
		}

	.hero-cinemagraph .cta-logo {
	   width: 40vw;
	}

	.hero-cinemagraph .m-wrap .content-box {
		width: 100%;
	}
}

@media (max-width: 767px) {

	 .hero-cinemagraph .cta-logo {
			width: 80vw;
		}

}

@media (max-width: 374px) {

 .hero-cinemagraph .cta-logo {
		width: 80vw
	}
}

2017-02-10 07:51:10
Teambuilding Notes

The team is understaffed. We need at least one more junior developer. William B has the potential to be a good lead, but needs both support and expectations set by studio management. He is overtasked - and accomplishes significant amoand needs the ability to delegat

Meetings are generally longer than they need to be, which makes people restless. Similarly, meetings have a tendency to go off-track. The probability that a meeting will be derailed is proportional to its duration. To constrain that, I'd go with a default meeting duration of 20 minutes. Anything longer than that needs to spend time up front (at the beginning of the meeting), going over a meeting agenda, to be included in the meeting request.

I am unclear of the role of the artists on the team. Both Marc and Doug are highly experienced, but I feel have gotten into a zone of "defensive design". I need them to extend their skillsets and provide not just new solutions, but new problems. case in point - designing in Photoshop is just a starting point, not the end-game. I

One of the points that came up in our retro was Doug's statement - "I don't know what I'm supposed to be doing on this team and I don't know who my boss is". It was a bold and honest statement that moved me.

There is a tendency at ZOS and other large studios to design for limitations and lowest common denominator situations. There's good reason for this - since the number of dependencies for any one thing can be large, and the ownership of these matters can be uncertain. As an example, I'd point to (a) the overreliance on un-altered screenshots used as promotional images and (b) the general lack of other types of promotional imagery - gifs, embedded video, embedded Web-GL.


Jo, here are the teambuilding notes you requested

-	whats working
Collaboration during implementation
Ongoing conversation between design and developers
Increasing usage of analytics to measure performance of designs
APE tool development

-	what's not
Perceived lack of ownership over final product
William B is overtasked. Should be working entoirely on features and tools
Relationship with marketing seems tense
QA is underutilized during design


-	what to watch
increased depth and scope of iterative work
provide simple tools and methods for artists to see their work in the browser
better usage of confluence for collaboration


This is a good team with certain dysfunctional issues that can be addressed to become a better team.

I've worked out basic details for handling sidebar content.
Prioritized sidebar content is exposed at the top of the page underneath the menu, with the possibility of pinning it in place.
Demonstrating this with a Recent News List
 - what does that look like it the list items are thumbnails?
 the reason for this is to provide an entry point to additional content up front to reduce bounce rate

 goal: reduce bouncerate by consistently placing breadcrumbs to other content
 goal: conversion
 depending on urgency, conversions should be
 (a) included in the body of the post
 (b) included in the mobile menu
 (c) included before the site menu (pushes the rest of the page down)
 (d) included in the sidebar
 (e) included after the page content

2017-04-20 17:03:35
I'm workinng out the use case for View More
- show all posts in long list
- show a more button that shows more posts, until all posts are shown in a long list on the same page
- pagination
	- older (previons n-listings on a new page)
	- newer (next n-listings on a new page)
	- indexed (collected in i-groups, each group opens a page with n-listings)

What are the benefits of keeping everything on the same page?
- simple navigation, just press more when needed, scroll backwards to see previous
- interaction is continuous - no page load. Hypothesis i s that this simplicity will cause more content to be listed (customers are encouraged to take deeper dive into the archive)

What are the drawbacks of keeping everything on the same page
- a long scroll complicates the behavior of the sidebar
	- it will need to be pinned to the side (as on pre-order page)
	- conversions keep getting pushed out of view when more content is made visible

What are the advantages of seperate pages
-	possibility to serve different banners on each page load

I'm not going in the right direction for resurfacing content in the archives above the primary post. In Most of the patterns I've examined, that resurfaced content is either
- pushed under the primary content
- included in the primary content
- hidden entirely
- moved behind a button/accordian which expands to show the resurfaced links

Simply showing them in a flat list above isn't going to work
- not scalable (for example, how would I handle the subject and history browsers on teh archive page)

No doubtthe changes (which look fantastic) seem disruptive. There's an open questioon going around about when we'll be done, and my gut says 3 months is what it would take to get v1 of the site comprehensively staged. WHle it felt awkwartd it seemedto get Jo B personally involved in the process.

My immediate next step is to finish the Guide Hub page as before, but with a new immediate outcome of putting together several page designs side by side, labeling the components, and sending that gfx to Jo in order to provide an overview of the larger patterns and to see the dupication of components.

2 candidates for moving forward with the timeline. Studying my work.

2017-05-10 17:33:03
setting up simple grid layout to validate timeline approach.
What are the Pros and COns?

GRID LAYOUT
Pro
	compact
	simple
	straightforward

Con
	difficult to see progression/differences between items
	no context or history
	doesnt distinguish well between DLC or Game updates
	uses Hub model - as if browsing arbitrary listing

TIMELINE LAYOUT
Pro
	Fun, rewards interaction (scrolling)
	Provides context and history
	clearly distinguishes between DLC and Updates
	uses funnel model - listing is biased towards most recent

Con
	expansive, unable to see the list in its entirely
	requires interaction to view (scrolling)

To the point about requiring scrolling, I feel that is actually a kind of engagement with the timeline.
There's not so much content in this list to make scrolling a chore
Similar lists exist in-game that are used (a) tactically and (b) with significantly greater frequency for longer durations

2017-05-10 18:47:28
basic look and feel established, other variations possible, be sure to call out usage of that specific tikeline script in the design doc

2017-05-10 22:38:52
completed 1st pass layout for wide view
- explains to customers how game updates work
- explains to customers how to get DLC
- pitches member benefits and provides CTA
- makes clear distinction between game packs (DLC) and updates
- provides feature list for each update
- provides immediate links to update/DLC pages (no scrolling required)
- rewards scrolling with a history lesson
- support for Tier-0 editorial block (with associated CTA)

what's missing
- could this listing be more compact?
	- don't want to sacrifice big images
	- only way to make list smaller is to remove feature listing or show only the gfx thumbnail
- could this metaphor be made visually stronger?
	- what about the Year headings I'd previoulsy looked at? bring them back?
- what's clickable?
	- hovering over an item wrapper puts it into a hilite state
	- hovering over a title puts it in a hilite state
		- also puts image into hilite state
		- links behave as normal
	- hovering over an image
		- also puts title into hilite state
		- links behave as normal
- are timeline nodes clickable?
	- I can see how they would be, they look like little buttons
	- making them selectable feels like overkill
- are timeline labels clickable?
	- clicking a date could open an archive page, but this defeats the purpose of a funnel
	- the only relevant destinations from this page are DLC pages, Buy Crowns, Become a Member

Next worksession
- add year headers to timeline
- replace pictures w existing gfx identity for each update
- responsive layout
- distribute mock to Slack discussion group
	- expect to hear about the length of the page
		- follow up with notes from above
- annotation and commentary in confluence

2017-05-11 08:01:02
Ryan's notes on ESO Plus from yesterday
NOTES:
 
ESO Plus Page goal: Clearly communicate the value of ESO Plus in a simple-to-understand, compelling way in effort to educate on the value prop and drive conversion.
 
Page requirements:
•	No age gate (paid media / mailers will drive here over time)
•	Clearly communicate the value of ESO Plus
•	Be built to scale over time as new benefits are added to the subscription
•	Facilitate FREE TRIAL periods w/ appropriate content changes to reflect
•	Copy
•	Static imagery
•	Video (potential)
•	Easily enable test & learn efforts
•	E.g., should allow us to embed an educational video or static image in a way that doesn't simply bury these assets
•	Should feel "premium" to the best of our ability
  
Future considerations:
•	How do we make the page feel more "premium", or reflect a "VIP experience"?
•	Should we eventually consider a member login version of this page to surface member-only info?
•	Maybe something akin to WoW Armory? Check out my beast-mode Paladin: https://worldofwarcraft.com/en-us/character/burning-legion/sammik

2017-05-16 09:51:59
auditing UX for current page

How do I arrive at this page
How do I return to this page?
What are the Membership Benefits?
- access to all DLC for the duration of the subscription
	- From inside the collections UI, players can select the DLC they wish to play and select the "Accept Quest" button
	- Selecting the "Accept Quest" button will put the player on the quest to get them into the DLC
- free Crowns to spend in the in-game Crown Store every month
- Access to Craft Bag, which lets you organize and store the hundreds of crafting materials you'll find in Tamriel
	- without this accessory, all crafting materials are stored in the limited player inventory
- Access to Costume Dyeing at any Dye Station
- Store 2x the normal amount of allowable fu7rnishing and collectibles in Player Housing
- 10% XP Bonus
- Find 10% more gold when looting corpses
- 10% bonus to Crafting Inspiration (craft better items)
- 10% Bonus to Trait Research (research more traits)

as stated on the current page:
x Access to DLC game packs¹
x Crowns to spend on the in-game store²
x Access to Costume Dyeing at Dye Stations
x Expanded crafting inventory3

1x 0% bonus to experience points gain¹
10% bonus to crafting inspiration gain¹
10% bonus to trait research time¹
10% bonus to gold acquisition¹
2x bonus to allowable furnishings and collectibles in player housing

What is the outcome of this page

2017-05-16 12:06:59
meeting about the Media page now

Requirements:
General:
  Being able to target a specific piece of media with a direct link.
  -	swtor.com/info/media
  Can we auto-watermark/brand overlay? Manage the mark in a single file so we wouldn't have to update a billion files when they do something like remove TU

Types:
Videos: YouTube playlist as a source

Wallpapers:
  Need to be able to get a direct link to a specific wallpaper instead of to the general wallpaper page.
  - Link should pull up the image with the option for all the available resolutions.
  - Upload zip file of all resolutions and it auto-generate links and resolutions.
  - Default preview image should be consistent. Can this be based on the user's screen resolution?

Concept: Jared/Art Team should curate the content.

Screenshots:
  Schedule timer for when they go live so we can obey exclusivity timing from PR.

 High level approach to this flow is that all media is treated as though it were a blog post
 it opens in a single post page which is directly linable and sharable
 media assets are tagged in the CMS w Title, description, and secondary description
 - these fields are searchable, but also sematically groupable

 Take a look at what Destinty is doing w YouTube video playlists exposed on their site

2017-05-23 11:35:59
the core problem to solve is to provide a destination for media URLs
the ability to target media with a direct link

2 pages for this design:
Media Hub
Media Post
Lightbox

Can likely repurpose the Single Page Post design to work for the Media Post page.
- if I were to do so, then what functionality would the page then need?

TITLE
AUTHOR, TIMESTAMP
SHARE

Secondary navigation
RECENT NEWS would become RECENT MEDIA

Conversions
Is there value in selling here?

Services
Is there value in newsletter signup here?

Think about it this way - the post page for media isn't large. The sidebar is likely to be larger than the main content (a problem?)
If I've cared enough to select a link to this page, what might I also care about?
- related media
	- image
	- video
	- playlist
- recent media (historical list)
- Media listed by subject
	- curated content

As long as I'm looking at pictures - why not sign up for an ESO Plus membership?
As long as I'm looking at pictures - why not signup for the newsletter
As long as I'm looking at pictures - why not buy crown store items
- image overload. too many images on the page at this point?

2017-05-24 09:09:20
working out details of the LOAD MORE lazy load interacrtion with Will

reference
https://www.crutchfield.com/m_11999/Home-Speakers.html

1. We've arrived at a Category page. Several product categories are listed here
https://www.crutchfield.com/m_11999/Home-Speakers.html

2. Select a category - let's say Floor-standing Speakers
3. Here is a list of floor standing speakers
https://www.crutchfield.com/g_12000/Floor-standing-Speakers.html?tp=185

4. We scroll to the bottom of the list and encounter the LOAD MORE PRODUCTS prompt
5. We select the LOAD MORE PRODUCTS prompt, and several more products are appended to the list
6. And again.
7. And again. This is a very long list of speakers
8. None of these are for us, lets go back to the Category page
9. We select the back button, andit returns us to the Category Page

scenario6 - featured video items + categorized playlists

P3 update prototype to jump to YouTube Playlist for designated items in the video gallery

1. the lightbox scales to fit the viewport
2. Removing Prev/Next from the Lightbox. Why?
	- I believe the prev/next pattern on lightboxes makes sense when repeated actions can be performed on the enries in the sequence.
	- For our purposes, navigating sequences in the lightbox removes customers from the broader context of browsing media
3. Selecting the Media Title opens the lightboxed media on a dedicated Media post page
4. Content
	- Video
	- Image
		- Wallpaper DL
			- resolutions are listed low to high
5. The lightbox centers all content horizontally and vertically in a given viewport
	- the media ales to fit the viewport
	- the media should never be scaled larger than its native size

				- The meteor
				- the demigod
				- Vivec City

	- in most existing DLC pages, this info is at the bottom of the page
	- this info is what I previoulsy used to build the Update Funnel Page
- more motion
  - animation of subject elements or of the narrative environment
  - we've got interesting UI animations in play - is that enough? (no)
  - embedded video header for everything
- Primary CTA is ESO PlUS
- Secondary CTA is Buy Crowns

2017-06-08 10:59:37
Page goal: Clearly communicate the value & benefits of a DLC pack in a digestible, compelling way to drive engagement and conversion.

Page requirements:
·         Succinctly communicate the benefits & value of DLC above the fold
·         Ideally use a short bullet list to communicate USPs
·         Include embedded video if possible
·         Ensure the page has a clean, polished look
·         Explore removing the munge effect
·         Make sure the flow is logical and that text / animations are well integrated throughout
·         Consider light-touch background animation for header (or other sections, if appropriate)
·         Ensure page CTAs & clickable elements are obvious, and don't compete for attention
·         ESO Plus should be the primary callout, over Crown Pack purchases
·         Enable A/B test efforts
·         Ability to A/B test designs, age gating, creative, CTAs, etc.
·         Ability to surface blog / community content related to DLC
·         Ability to include Crown Store items, if deemed useful

2017-06-08 15:45:44
I'm back on the guides page addressing stakeholder feedback and futher ideation. The mistake made previously may have been to anthropomorhize the humans (my segmenting between new and returning players)

The solution I'm working on extends the sidebar to behave as frame-based navigation for the guide system in full. Its a categorized accordion list that can be color coded and tokenized furthder to indicate context. Guide Pages are also shown here - is a 2 column list/preview pattern.

I'm also addressing inter-guide linking by calling out all player guides linked to within a particular guide.
The idea feels sound to me, probably more computer-y than I'd have anticipated, but its efficient and as I was reminded earlier, this is also what we do in-game.

The question to me then becomes, what do these guides do that the game guides do bnot? And i thin k - ease of use and interlinking must be a big part of that

The Justice System

2017-06-08 17:45:02
as a side note, the tone of these guides seems too detailed and technical - at the very least we need screenshots as callout gfx. As someone who knows the game, I'm finding the descriptions hard to follow.

The way to express that is this
What do Player Guides on the website offer that in-game help does not?
- hyperlinked content (browsing)
- detailed descriptions
- rich media

What does in-game help offer that the Player Guides can not?
- immediacy (because you're already in the game)
- context (because you're already in the game)

Some of the content feels quite readable, and gives me a sense of purpose - in particular, The Getting Started Guides. However some of the guides are detailed step by step instructions for things that happen in the game. For material like this, its imperative to provide visual reference. The images currently in place are non-specific. This type of image makes narrative copy come alive. We need (a) images to support the text, or (b) rethink the text.

I'll use the Justice System as an example.
From a personal standpoint, I'm more interested in the question Why Murder?" than "How do I murder?"
Specifically, what are the benefits of being an outlaw, what are the consequences.

TL;DR
1. Player guides lack images to guide customers through the detailed steps listed

2017-06-14 09:08:01
HOMESTEAD

Your Home is Waiting for You

After a long day of questing, everyone needs a place to unwind. With *dozens of homes* available at launch, there's something for everyone in Homestead, The Elder Scrolls Online's latest update. To get you started, you can earn your first home free as a unique quest reward.

A HOME TO SUIT YOUR TASTES

Be close to Queen Ayrenn with a humble apartment in Skywatch or cozy up to the hearth fire in a grand Nord estate. With architectural styles inspired by each playable race and 39 unique abodes varying in size from humble one-bedroom cottages to gigantic aristocratic mansions, you'll find the perfect place to call home. Can't decide which place you like more? You can own them all! Each home is shared across all your characters on your account.

Make Your House a Home

With over 2,000 decorative and other furnishings, you can design the interior of your dreams. Fill your home with trophies from conquered bosses, luxury items found through in‑game vendors, or browse the Crown Store's Housing Editor for a wide variety of furnishings. You can even craft your own furniture to decorate your home, or sell them to make a nice profit.

Explore Your Social Side

Invite your friends over to share tales of Tamriel, prepare for a dungeon run by practicing against Target Skeletons, give decorator permissions to your friends and build something together, or simply sit down and relax in your customized space. Whatever the occasion, share the company of your companions in the comfort of your own home.

Available Now

Homestead
	Dozens of homes available with unique architectural styles
	Thousands of items to customize your space
	Brand new crafting system to build furniture as decoration or to sell
	Invite friends and guildmates over to your home

Don't worry, all homes are secure! Other players are not able to break into your household to steal your items. (Sorry, Thieves Guild!)

Goals:
Show DETAILS First

2017-06-17 18:28:15
To control T0 blocks -
content should default to anchoring to the top of the frame, which keeps it consistently viewable in wide viewports.
I'm recognizing that centered anchoring is just harder to parse when the page expands
Similarly - the question about placing text above or beneath a wrapper - answers itself. placement at the top of an expanding wrapper will be more consistently viewable

2017-06-22 14:36:15
When designed to delight and direct users, 404 pages can transform from sources of frustration to opportunities for conversion and branding
A standard, out-of-the box 404 page gives users only one option – go back to where they came from.
A lot of sites used to fall into the trap of “Panic! The user got a 404! Let’s show them everything we got and see if they bite” Bad idea. Overwhelming the user with a 404 page jam-packed with links to every nook and cranny of your site is a surefire way to cognitively overload an already frustrated user.
Best practice is to design a 404 page with a handful of key links, including a link to the Home page as an easy get-out for users who’re not sure what they’re looking for yet. Other options to include might be to a blog, product features or About Us.
For many users, a 404 page might be the very first interaction they have with a brand. That’s a lot of pressure for one little error page.
404 error pages used to be a huge source of user frustration and abandonment. But it doesn’t have to be that way. Increasingly UI/UX designers are seeing 404s as a way to keep users engaged, introduce their brand and solve user problems.

2017-07-26 08:24:03
why am I making this so hard?
For one thing, I'm trying to solve layouts for 2 systems at once - Guide Pages, Crown Store, and possibly archive pages.
How about solving just for the Crown Store and building outward.
For another thing, its non-trivial.
For another thing, I'm feeling (imagined? real?) time pressure from the team and am beating myself up about taking the time to get the design right (which is the goal, by the way)

Advice. You need to depend on others more. Stand up the best that you can, and open iuup tyhe discussion to the possibilities.

Why isn't this a regular dropdown?
- because the dropdowns are limited in styling and content
- I want to be able to show the full accordion and  possibly history navigator here.

What are the red flags you're coming across?
The targets in the sidebar controls seem small when exposed as a menu.
Its quite likely the layout of these items (sizing, whitespace) should chamnge in response to viewport size. If they're small targets on the menu, they're small targets on the page.


2017-07-26 08:47:33
I'm arriving there. Feeling better about the mechanism where page content isn't replaced, just covered. I saw this pattern on the CNN mobile site on my phone

2017-08-01 10:20:27
site launch today
as expected, feedback in the Forums was pretty direct - at least as it related to how Universal Navigation integrates with Forums. The issue that keeps coming up again and again is that the header is too tall. As I recall, Marketing had similar concers, so it should not be difficult to devote resources to addressing this. Spent some time w Cory and Erich working through design ideas to (a) remove the collapsing functionality on scroll for the current nav (b) retain the product name and brand identity in this restricted space. We came up with enough plausible ideas that I hope this will be a quick fix


2017-08-01 10:23:18
Along with my invoice I intend to send out a letter to Matt F, Jo Burba, Ryan, Jordan, and Hillary to call out the positive contributions made by the web team:
Cory Tsang
William Brall
Greg Fick
Dominic Davies
Shawn Lindsay
Doug Wilkins

Also call out that although the obvious artifact is the website, our design and development goes further than that. Today, we are looking at our Tier-1 implementation. Tier-2 will be released in a month which includes updates for:
- The Crown Store
- DLC/Product Pages
- Buy Crowns
- Search

Point out the work (dev and UX) done on the content management system (APE) which:
- simplifies page creation
- simplifies blog post creation
- provides new media management tools
- new Crown Store management tools
- provides modular functionality (navigation, promotion, sharing) at all levels of content authoring
	- translation: content can be stood up, refined and tested more quickly than ever
- emphasizes incoming links from social shares, PR, marketing
	- crown store items will each have a unique page, which can be reached directly by an external mention, for example - facebook
	- media assets now have a unique page, which can be reached directly by an external mention, for example - facebook

2017-08-06 13:48:54
after discussion with the team here are a couple of things I can consider to be anchors:
1. Search Query can be presented as a modal dialog
2. Search results can be surfaced within the Query Modal (but is a UX requirement to think that though
3. Search Results pages aren't the same as Archive results pages
	- broader immediate requirements for search, namely each results page needs to be addressable as a destination through a URL
	- as a Tier-3 feature, it is worth re-examining how Archive Results and Search Results can be generalize to be simply "Results"
4. Do not include boilerplate queries or links in the Query phase
	- was asked to do so, but universal pushback against doing so

The design document I am standing up deliberately seperates the query phase from the results phase. If its felt that adding results to the query phase is the preferred choice, I'm not against doing so either. The larger issue is presenting those outcomes more smoothly. The current implementation gests us there functionally, but is an awkward presentation.

2017-08-09 15:50:35

min-width in media queries is not related to the min-width property you set on elements, those are two different things.

In media queries min-width: X is true if the viewport has a width greater or equal to X, effectively working as screen.width >= X

1200
768
500
400
0

2017-08-16 11:32:51
Crown store feedback

CATEGORIZATION

How do I get back to Featured items after entering a category?

2017-09-13 10:49:53

We launched a new game site! I hope you like it. In the process, we re-invented how to manage, produce and validate the editorial materials we provide - significantly more agile. Thank you for the opportunity to extend my vision for ESO into this space. I'd like to call out the contributions of my colleagues on the web team - especially Cory Tsang, William Brall, Shawn Lindsey, Gregory Fick, Dominic Davies and Eric Buhlman.

My current contract is effective until 9/30. I want to extend that and continue the work we've launched.

Here is a brief summary of the initiatives where I can contribute value:

PRODUCT SITE (elderscrollsonline.com)
Post-redesign, my ongoing involvement with the web team includes:
- Design direction for Marketing, Promotion and New Features
- UX Refinement
- HTML/CSS development
- Site Maintenance and Documentation

ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT
We've started foundational work in the area of Account Management that will benefit customer onboarding, customer service, and of course revenue. I can help make that happen.

There are 2 outstanding design spaces that we've spoken about but have not had the bandwidth to deliver.

THE PATCHER
This has needed a product refresh for some time, and I'd previously stood up wireframes to update this design. The modular approach behind the website redesign can be applied in this space as well.

STUDIO WEBSITE (zenimaxonline.com)
Matt, we'd spoken about your interest in refocusing this site on recruiting. This is a wonderful oportunity for me to stretch my legs creatively and scale my business services.

All the best.

AC for this task are pretty vague. What can I do to clarify?

AC: Sale Text Area w/ Multi-Country Support
- Additional text area in the left column to call out sale messaging. It will push down the box art when text is visible.
- A wysiwyg text box that can be edited based on Country/platform/sku/language
- Set a default for all countries then be able to override specific ones.
- Supports all languages. Will mainly utilize (US, CA, UK, FR, DE, RU, AU, BR, PL)
- Toggle switch to turn on/off all
- Toggle switch to turn on/off individual countries.

- Sale messaging (gfx)
- Sale messaging (text)

I've become familiar with optin monster CSS manipulation, and am coming to the realization that for me, its a prototyping tool. It is possible to finish the design in 4h. Here's how:
- use optin monster as a layout guide
- capture screen metrics
- capture bkg behavior
- transfer to AX for layout at rapid design iteration
	- what are the use cases being described in Eric's doc?
		+ Limited time offer, members only
		+ Product on sale
		+ Newsletter signup

- P3 the HTML testbed I made today can be used to explore questions of timing and

The mininav may not be strictly necessary for wider views, but helps navigate long scroll with narrow views

- I'm going to try adding a special condition to a mininav item
	- plausible
- I'm going to try adding a special condition to a section header item
	- overkill

for the meeting around this doc here's the outcomes I want

DISCUSSION
It's not a visual design task yet.
Do we need to re-invent the wheel entirely or could we adapt the existing site to meet requirements and add new pages as necessary?
What's wrong with the visual identity of the bethesda template vs. some of the other site examples?
going by the metrics I identified in the site comparisomn, rank those values for our purposes. what's most important and why
Help me make up "facts" about the Employee persona
	- ask for data before meeting and create persona(s) from that sample

FEATURES
Scope out the cost and value of each content type.
Identify Tier-1, Tier-2 features.
Identify missing features
Identify what we can't/won't do

For each high ranking feature, map out the creative dependencies. How long would it take to do one instance of it? What would a rough prototype of this look like?

THEME

2017-12-13 15:55:59
Just reviewed with Jordan. Additional notes here…

·        To further simplify the Edition Seletion nav, we think it makes sense to remove “upgrade” versions from this bar. From there, we’d like Gary to explore a solve to ensure that, upon selecting Glacier standard, Digital Deluxe, or CE, players can then select whether they want “full game + Glacier” or just the Glacier upgrade of that SKU type.
·        Upon further review of the design, we believe the section currently reserved for pre-order / CE bonus items can also be effectively used to communicate core features of the product (screenshot + small blurb of text). However:
o   If we go this route, would like to find a way to visually distinguish:
§  Product features
§  Pre-order incentives
§  Digital goods associated with a product SKU (e.g., the CE version and items that come with it)
·        We’d like to test “Buy Now” button colors ahead of Glacier release, to ensure the color we use at that point is proven to be the best route forward. Currently it’s green in the design doc.
·        Regarding the main asset image (currently Morrowind physical CE): need a solve for what this looks like when we’re simply featuring a box shot here. Not all SKUs will have an asset large enough to warrant the space that’s currently allotted.

2017-12-14 11:48:46
spoke w Ryan and Eric seperately to determine course of action and get some clarification on the Buy Now revisions. Took notes in AX as we spoke, so transcribing here

SECONDARY EDITION SELECTOR
	The goal is to simplify selection by segmenting it into multiple choices.
	For a specific SKU, for example "Morrowind" there may be a number of related product packages, such as: "Full Game", "Upgrade"

	Rather than presenting these offerings inline at the Sales Window (which increases the number of apparent choices when combined with other SKUS - each of which may have multiple versions)

	Morrowind Full Game | Morrowind Upgrade | SKU1 Package 1 | SKU1 Package 2 | SKU1 Package 3

	Present these as a 2-stage choice w preselected choice as default
	Morrowind
		x Collector's Edition
		Full Game
		Upgrade

	SKU1
		x Package 1
		Package 2
		Package 3

GAME FEATURES
	identify game features and present seperately from Edition bonuses

PLATFORM SPECIFIC FEATURES
	This will be a text blurb, presented as an alert/marquee section above the hero image and edition title.
		Any platform specific infor to be called out by the editorial of thiis text

PRE-FOOTER CTA
	- place a CTA at the bottom of the page.
	- this should probably just auto-scroll to top of page, and be labeled as such (?)

TASKS
- create secondary edition selector
	+ what does it look like on page entry
	+ how does this selection affect the content matrix

- update content matrix to use new edition selection language
	Battlechest
	Morrowind Full Game
	Morrowind Upgrade
	Glacier Full Game
	Glacier Upgrade
	Standard Edition

- create section for game features
- create a section for sku-specific messaging
	+ provide example of using the text box to call out platform details

2017-12-21 07:51:55
Here is Ryan's consolidated feedback, some of which I've already addressed
From: Ryan Taljonick
Sent: Wednesday, December 20, 2017 3:13 PM
To: Gary Boodhoo; Cory Tsang; Eric Buhlman
Cc: Jordan Rosenbaum
Subject: Buy Now page - consolidated feedback

Hey Gary,

Wanted to send all consolidated feedback to you on the latest draft of the Buy Now page. In general, awesome stuff here. Looking great. The highlighted below are new notes since my post in Slack yesterday.

PRODUCT IMAGE
For the product image that appears at the top of the page: hoping we can actually turn this into a carousel. Would be great to have the opportunity to show a few screenshots at the top here, in addition to the product shot itself (similar to what Amazon does on its product pages).

THE SITE STRIPE
this is absolutely a step in the right direction! Two quick concerns:
- Text needs to be bigger. I know it seems already obvious given the color treatment, but we’ll definitely want this to be un-missable. Right now it kinda blends in with the page a bit too much.
- Red color might be better suited for something more thematic to Glacier, but obviously we don’t  have much to go off of yet at this point. Just want to ensure we have flexibility to easily change color here.

DETAILS SECTION
we currently have "About this edition" and "about this game" as options. We'll need an additional one around pre-order bonuses. In terms of which gets displayed by default, and what order they appear, would like that order to be:
- Pre-order Bonuses
- About this Edition (can we edit text to read “Collector’s Edition Content”
- About the Game (can we edit text to read “Game Features”)
- Further – can you confirm these images are expandable? If I click on a “Grey Bear” CE bonus item, does it blow up in a light box?

SECONDARY SELECTOR
- At the top where you can select full game vs. upgrade - let's change that more to a toggle than a check box system, from a visual POV. Checkboxes make me think i can select more than one


COMPARE EDITION:
Let’s highlight these SKUs, from left to right:

- Glacier Upgrade
- Glacier Standard (includes base game)
- The Elder Scrolls Online (includes base game + Morrowind chapter)
- Battle Chest (includes everything on planet earth)

2018-01-03 14:39:47
going back over the beginner tutorial

each SSP template is made of several template files.
Every template must have at minimum, a region file (usually named site.region) and a template config file, named template.conf

Templates fall into 3 categories
1. site-wide files
2. collection-specific files
3. block-specific files

site.region
	- defines layout of template pages
	- typically used as the global site wrapper
		+ containing site header
		+ site footer
		+ sidebar
	- typically used as the default layout
	- composed of HTML and JSON-T

template.conf
	- uses JSON to config elements within the template
	- name your template
	- specify layouts
	- add navigation sections
	- specify stylesheets to be rolled up into site.css

/assets
	- static design assets
		+ images
		+ fonts
		+ icons

/blocks
	- block files
	- JSON-T "partials"
		- reusable code that can be included elsewhere in your JSON-T
		- like a macro, I suppose

/collections
	- collection files
		- (collection).list
			+ list view of the collection is default view
			+ sets up structure of how a list will look
			+ HTML/JSON-T
		- (collection).item
			+ sets up striucture of how single post will look
			+ HTML/JSON-T
		- (collection).conf
			+ tells squarespace platform how each collection is configured
			+ JSON

/pages
	- static page files
		- (static).page
		- (static).page.conf

/scripts
	- javascript files
		- site.js

/styles
	- stylesheets
		- styles.css
		- styles.less

(root)
	- sitewide files
		- site.region
		- template.conf

	+ define success
	+ keep it simple/obvious. don't overthink it

2018-01-06 17:52:57
Completed the creative brief. I have a basic idea that I am ready to start sketching out in AX


THEME: INVENTOR
ZOS values are largely unspoken. Digging into that culture and packaging it visually to meet Matt’s goal of "legitimacy" is the challenge. Ideas guiding this visual design theme center around the company's unique AAA product

1. Many Paths
	- Provide depth by offering many paths to content
	- Use a sidebar for secondary navigation
	- Use a sidebar to persistently expose key site goals by showing:
		+ featured jobs
		+ featured employees
		+ departments
2. Compact Complexity
	- This is a dashboard not a magazine
		- Restrict page height by keeping sections compact
		- Hero images span screen width
			+ text doesn't ever cover these hero images
	- Use sidebar/tabs/clickables to selectively show/hide info
3. Legacy and Roots
	- This is a dark game with breathtaking contrast between extremes
		+ start from dark base color, use color as an accent
	- This game has a legacy stretching back to the 1990's
		+ Incorporate tropes and patterns from text-driven RPG's

THE IDEA:
The display is divided by a persistent sidebar which collapses into a clickable widget cloating above scrolling content. The sidebar is a dashboard where key site goals (featured jobs, employees, department list, primary CTA) are always exposed. The primary panel shows relevant page content, including the footer and any secondary section navigations. It expands in width when the sidebar collapses.

GOAL:
- Encourage exploration and reward curiosity

MEASURING SUCCESS:
- I am able to give the design a meaningful codename that stakeholders can immediately grasp
	+ working title: Dashboard/ControlPanel/Instrument
- Clickthrough to job search
- Clickthrough to deeper site links
- Provide enough interesting content here to extend single pageview

reading through chapter page PO notes
https://confluence.zenimaxonline.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=78940637

Not revamping DLC pages. Enhancing the current design over a rebuild

Goals:
	Excite players that come here
	Visually appealing
Surface the information they are looking for to understand what the chapter is
	Educate
	Give players reason to consume more of the page.

What does it mean to excite?
	T0 spot is doing the job
		- although seeing greater need for flewxibility in this component
			+ text alignment - upper, lower, left, right, center
			+ multicolumn layouts
			+ Scrolling experience - existing is doing the job

	Need more interesting transitions (between sections)
		Ideas
			Theming
				Themed dividers
				Themed buttons
				Consider other themed components (video player, image borders)
				Examples: Monster Hunter and WoW
			Image overlay connects one to the next (WoW)

	Adiitional thoughts and requirements
		Game Map section
			shows the new area in the game

		Integrate more movement on the page
			GIF's
			CSS anim?
			glows
			shiny
			parallax scrolling?

	Tabbed Panels
		in depth info about skill lines

KPI's
	Time on Page (more time = more education)
	Main Conversions to the buy page from any buy links on the page
		Retailer link Clicks
	Micro Conversions: Interaction with the various pieces of media on the page
		Videos clicks
		Button clicks

Eric's thoughts on page structure
- Heading hero shot w/ hook
- What's new
- Location
- Skill Line
- Characters
- Base game stuff

2018-01-17 16:25:24
working on creative brief for Chapter Page redesign

Here's the template for a creative brief:

THEME: CHAPTER2
Each chapter of the game brings new features and worlds to explore. This page is much a guide as it is an advertisement. For new and existing customers alike, this page is the first stop to learn what the new release brings to the game.

PRODUCT OWNER SUMMARY
1. Excitement
	- Better transitions between sections (visual rhythm)
	- Examine the usage of motion assets on the page
	- Examine the usage of motion effects related to scrolling
2. Depth
	- Add new sections
		+ What's New
		+ Map
		+ Skill Lines
		+ Characters
		+ Game features
	- Provide details about skills
3. Improvements
	- An opportunity to more rigorously apply visual language standards
	- Expanded functionality for section layouts on the page and CMS

THE IDEA:
The display is divided by a persistent sidebar which collapses into a clickable widget cloating above scrolling content. The sidebar is a dashboard where key site goals (featured jobs, employees, department list, primary CTA) are always exposed. The primary panel shows relevant page content, including the footer and any secondary section navigations. It expands in width when the sidebar collapses.

DESIGN GOAL:
- Encourage exploration by helping customers understand the new chapter and make it a pleasant journey

MEASURING SUCCESS:
- Clickthrough to Buy Now
- Time spent on page
- Clicks on Video Assets

Queen Ayrenn
At a mere 28 years old, Queen Ayrenn is young, especially for an Altmer. She is also known as the "Unforeseen Queen", having mysteriously disappeared from Summerset as a child, only to return 17 years later so she could inherit the Throne of Alinor.(1)(2) Shortly after her coronation, Ayrenn formed the First Aldmeri Dominion, an alliance between the Summerset Isles, Valenwood and Elsweyr which faced opposition from both the Daggerfall Covenant, the Ebonheart Pact, and even her own people in Summerset

New zone: The Summerset Isles
New Psijic Order skill line
Addition of jewelry crafting
New trial: Cloudrest
New armor and weapon sets
New delves
New public dungeons
New world bosses
New collectibles
New crafting motifs

2018-02-08 10:27:13

All sections EXCEPT jewlery crafting will have an associated video

For the "Continue your adventure" section, some things we wanna highlight are the following, each with an image, header title, and likely 80-100 characters of description text: Razum-dar, Queen Ayrenn, and the Court of Bedlam (a sinister organization) This section will eventually include a video as well. when i say header title, i just mean a bolded line of text to distinguish the character's name vs. their description text. In terms of layout - i'm thinking that 3-stack, where you had one image + text block left aligned, then one right-aligned, then a third left-aligned. Additionally, 2 more things we'l;l likely want to highlight in the "continue your adventure" section: Ritemaster Iachesis (a key psijic order character) and the Yagrha, new monsters. Might be worth exploring what that section looks like if we highlight 3-6 things total

For the Psijic Order section, would like to highlight the Psijic Order themselves (as an organization)
the island of Artaeum (their home, which they can teleport through time and space. crazy, i know)
and finally the Psijic Order SKill Line
for this section, i think it may make more sense to simpyl use that layout of 3 images + text descriptions side by side
again each with a title line, and 80-100 chars of description
This section will also have a video to use at some point, though not at page go-live




Finally, the Jewlery Crafting section will be the simplets of the grou
it'll just be the USP description text, for the most part. Maybe 80-100 chars of extra flavor text, if that helps that section feel less sparse

doing some tweaking of the redbox on Buy Now page


2018-02-13 09:59:46
a couple of tweaks I want to see:
  COLOR
I want greater emphasis on the red, and better definition of the redbox

-- icons on buttons
@media (min-width: 0px)
.buy-now-top .btn-buy-now-sm .fa, .buy-now-top .btn-buy-now-sm-alt .fa {
    color:  C00;
}

-- redbox
@media (min-width: 0px)
.buy-now-top .top-section .red-box {
    background: rgba(128, 0, 0, .75);
    border-radius: 3px;
    -moz-border-radius: 3px;
    -webkit-border-radius: 3px;
    border: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.2);
    border-left: 5px solid  c00;
    border-right: 5px solid  c00;
    padding: 30px;
    overflow: hidden;
    margin: 0 15px;
    -webkit-box-shadow: 0px 1px 1px 0px rgba(0,0,0,0.25);
    -moz-box-shadow: 0px 1px 1px 0px rgba(0,0,0,0.25);
    box-shadow: 0px 1px 1px 0px rgba(0,0,0,0.25);
}

  SPACING

-- fatten up button selectors and add some inner whitespace
@media (min-width: 0px)
.buy-now-top .btn-buy-now-sm, .buy-now-top .btn-buy-now-sm-alt {
    color:  C5BF9E;
    font-family: "Einstein Futura PT Cond",sans-serif;
    height: 38px;
    line-height: 29px;
    padding: 4px 12px;
    font-size: 20px;
    background:  151511;
    text-shadow: initial;
    margin: 0 5px 10px 0;
    border-width: 1px;
}

-- tighter spacing between button selectors (vertical & horizontal)
@media (min-width: 0px)
.buy-now-top .btn-buy-now-sm, .buy-now-top .btn-buy-now-sm-alt {
    color:  C5BF9E;
    font-family: "Einstein Futura PT Cond",sans-serif;
    height: 31px;
    line-height: 29px;
    padding: 0 8px;
    font-size: 18px;
    background:  151511;
    text-shadow: initial;
    margin: 0 5px 10px 0;
    border-width: 1px;
}

however - this reduces the vertical spacing between top selectors and page content to 10px. I want to keep that at 20px

TECH REVIEW TALKING POINTS
- I've identified 3 main section types (not including the news section)
	+ primary cover
		- assumes a significant focal area for bkg image
		- keeps primary CTA above the "fold"
		- 1 column layout by default
	+ basic cover
		- assumes a significant focal area for bkg image
		- expresses a single idea concisely with header & subhead
		- 2 column layout by default
			- special cases where additional content must be shown, conforms that content to a single column
	+ index section
		- uses bkg as wallpaper, focus is on foreground content
		- 2 column layout by default
- I've specified layout for a MdLg view that is 1024px wide. This corresponds to tablet in landscape orientation
- I've designed the distances between parts to be divisible by 8
	- 8, 16, 24, 32, etc.
	+ will demonstrate that for each section type in further design annotation

2018-02-21 17:25:41
Here are Ryans previous notes

Hey Gary,



Per our discussion the other day on last chapter page edits needed:



Include transition elements, even if they’re minor.
	- Black lines are hard transitions won’t work.
		- Would like to see Creative Services solve this visual element
			+ Provide design spec.
			+ put this together as if communicating to a manager, and then to a production artist
Want to make sure the Jewelry Crafting section is updated/expanded to speak to new PvE content, specifically:
	- Jewelry crafting
	- New trial
	- New delves & world bosses
	- content TBD, use dummy text here
Want to re-order the page so the sections are:
	- Summerset locations
	- Psijic Order
	- Key Characters
	- New PvE Content


2018-02-21 17:46:01

NEXT
- Communicate Ryan's updates to Eric before Scrum
- Create page template for new PvE Content section
	+ this is a variation of a 2-col layout already used, ETA 1.5h
- page audit for visual design
	+ discuss metrics, layout, typography w Will
- meet w ZOS team & Jordan to discuss work in progress
	+ communicate Ryan's updates here


There's a strong desire among the webteam to refactor and simplify/unify our presentation. When we look at the site, we're seeing different levels of achievement. Simply put, we're better at what we do now than then - I hope that the recent work on Buy Now & the new Chapter Promo Page will speak to that.

We find ourselves special-casing certain parts of the design spec, and ask ourselves - why not just update the spec?


Hey Jordan, I want to be sure I'm able to responsibly be of service. I got a bit emotional, and let me apologize - it is not my style of working.
Here's how I think we can approach this, after some discussion w Eric.

Tier-1
Typography refinement
- unify type sizing and styling across the site
	+ minimize or remove any spcial cases that have come up over the course of development
- unify type sizing across all viewports: phone, tableet, desktop, ultrawide
	+ all text links should be the same size by default (special cases exist)
- color coding
	+ reduce contrast ratio of font coloring
		* gold may be too saturated/bright
		* body text may be too grey/dark
		* when does header textreally need to have their own color?
- unify color coding for all CTA's
	+ we currentkly use at least 3 styles. There should only be one.


Tier-2
Content refinement
This will take some further analysis, but aims to reduce apparent complexity and footprint of a page. Some initial ideas
	- we don't need to show a thumbnail and excerpt text for every news item, just the featured/most recent
		+ CNN or any larger site provides a good example of how to compress the news
	- we don't need to fill up the sidebar at all times
	- crown items in the sidebar can be shown smaller
		+ 2-col layout?
		+ smaller icon + one line text label?
	- consider redesigning game updates page
		+ stands out as very different than other pages
		+ or show game updates + links on the front page (?)

2018-03-27 10:57:24
INTERVIEW NOTES: DAVID CAMPBELL
Graphic Artist at Whole Foods
Formerly Brand & Communications Design Manager at Common Market Coop
- what were the responsibilities and challenges of this position
- reimagined this brand. how did you begin? who were the stakeholders

Portfolio Notes
- competent designer. confident. nice emphasis on illustration
- sign maker, independent comic artist
- seems cool. super honest
- expertise is in print
- active and popular on Behance
- gamer
- presented print portfolio in thematic game case w CD

Self-Education
1. Is he familiar with the term responsive design?
2. if he had to create the layout for a responsive website in 2 weeks, how would you he go about it

	- looking for resourcefulness, time management, strategy, willingness to learn
	- hoping to hear that he would ask for help and not go it alone
	- hoping to hear that he'd ask someone to review his work
	- hoping to hear that he'd use widely available tools (squarespace, wordpress, templates of any kind) to solve the problem quickly

SUMMARY: DAVID CAMPBELL
liking this guy. He's thoughtful and confident. Knows what he's talking about and thinks about the reasons and politics behind the designs he works on. Could be more of a hacker, but he provided a reasonable solution to my question addressing resourceful to figure out a responsive design in 2 weeks. Was hoping to hear him use a templated solution to start getting feedback early, or asking for help. Admired his honesty and full disclosure about looking for a job because of impending layoffs at Whole Foods. Great printed promo portfolio presented in game case and optical disc. badass

INTERVIEW NOTES: DALLAS ABRAMSON
Studied Psychology in School
	- how does background in psychology inform his work?
MarketingManager at Corbin Salon
- what were the responsibilities and challenges of this position
- how did you grow the business

Portfolio Notes
- average designer, involved in social causes
- expertise is in print

Self-Education
1. Is he familiar with the term responsive design?
2. if he had to create the layout for a responsive website in 2 weeks, how would you he go about it

	- looking for resourcefulness, time management, strategy, willingness to learn
	- hoping to hear that he would ask for help and not go it alone
	- hoping to hear that he'd ask someone to review his work
	- hoping to hear that he'd use widely available tools (squarespace, wordpress, templates of any kind) to solve the problem quickly

SUMMARY: DALLAS ABRAMSON
Brought up asking questions to get direction.
Uses a tool called brackets.
Taught self how to code using notepad
Making informed statements about how to setup a responsive design
Great answers on the border dividers - code driven, described strategies for show and tell using photoshop cut/paste as well as developer console in browser
Calls out contributors to his portfolio
More of a mature designer than I expected from his portfolio and resume.
Good production art skills - nailed the 50 image cutout question
Good candidate.



2018-03-29 10:10:53
What’s an example of a project where you disagreed with the client’s feedback and how did you handle it?

INTERVIEW NOTES: Brian Boehm
Has been designing for past 20 years
Undergraduate psychology degree
	- how does background in psychology inform his work?
Clever resume
Has won several awards related top book design
Agency and contract work as an idependent contractor
Broad experience with infographics, logos, trade show booths, banners, magazines, websites and package designs

Portfolio Notes
- empty blog on portfolio site
	+ what was he planning to put there?
- average designer, involved in social causes
- expertise is in print
	+ what has kept him involved with print for the past 20 years?
	+ What is it about print that attracts him as a designer
	+ What are the differences between designing for print and designing for the web

Self-Description
- Brian describes himself as a strategist. Ask him to expand upon that description. How do those skills come into play in his work
- How does Brian respond to feedback and incorporate it into his design projects?
	+ what if the feedback is bad?
- How does Brian sell his ideas to a team?
- Brian has been designing for 20 years. What advice would he give to himself in 1994?
- Brian's first job lasted for 10 years. What kept him there?
- Brian describes a process for vendor communication. Ask him to detail that process further
- What is motivating Brian to shift from a freelance to a salaried position?


Self-Education
1. Is he familiar with the term responsive design?
2. if he had to create a responsive layout for a promotional website in 2 weeks, how would you he go about it
	3. How would he gather requirements?
	4. How would he gather assets?
	5. How would he evaluate the work on different platforms and devices

	- looking for him to ask about the scope/size of the site
		+ landing page, product feature, about us, contact form
		+ client desires tight visual comp to sell and promote idea to executives who will provide funding
		+ Call to Action is to get email address

	- looking for emphasis on content over layout
	- looking for resourcefulness, time management, strategy, willingness to learn
	- hoping to hear that he would ask for help and not go it alone
	- hoping to hear that he'd ask someone to review his work
	- hoping to hear that he'd use widely available tools (squarespace, wordpress, templates of any kind) to solve the problem quickly

My concern with this candidate is that he has remained local and arguably "played it safe" for the past 20 years. He makes no secret of this, describing his personal commitments to family, etc. In his bio. Is this a fair assessment?
- what is his level of expertise with HTML/CSS
- how did he learn this skill

SUMMARY: BRIAN BOEHM
Seems like Brian either burned out or gained new priorities - a family, then found his way back in. Detailed technical knowlegde of print technologies with emphaiss on communicating w vendors and previz. Had a mentor at Parkton Art (his first job) Met with and formed relationships with rinters, heard inside stores that led him to rigorous work
No experience working in-browser. however excited about prospect of working on the site for a game he plays. Hoping for regular schedule, consistent team. Follows up with clients face to face to determine psychological profile. Describes a back and forth process. Intelligent and experienced approach to client management and time management.

2018-04-04 07:39:14
observing how squarespace handles the employee listing. It does what I had thought to do, and will continue with - the content is as long as it needs to be - doesn't get truncated at all. The row height is the height of the tallest item in the row plus any padding. If the text for the item is short, or nonexistent the behavior is the same


2018-04-04 10:12:41
rebuilding the employee list so that department is shown at the bottom of a panel whose height is determined by the tallest entry in a row. Will need to figure out how to do this with dev. ZeniMax Online Studios is a premier developer and worldwide publisher of online entertainment software.

Located in Hunt Valley, Maryland, they are continuing development on their hit multiplayer roleplaying game, The Elder Scrolls Online: Tamriel Unlimited for PC, Mac, PlayStation4, and Xbox One. Subsequent updates have included a Morrowind expansion in June 2017, numerous DLCs and updates, as well as the upcoming Summerset expansion available June 2018. Elder Scrolls Online has been named Online Game and MMO of the Year for the past 3 years in a row.

WORK WITH US
ZeniMax Online is hiring for both Elder Scrolls Online DLC and ongoing updates, as well as for unannounced projects. We're looking for talented, self-motivated teammates with the skills and passion to drive innovation. Join us in our quest to connect gamers to virtual worlds, and to each other!


Art
Audio
Customer Support
Design
Production
Programming

2-col layout for body text in header section is a good pattern to follow which keeps line lengths readable at wider viewports. This is in contrast to the pattern of reducing line lengths by increasing column offsets and "sandwiching that content"

HEADING: WE CHOOSE MARYLAND
·         Sample Copy: Located in the heart of the US East Coast, Maryland is the ideal place to start a family, a career, or both. Whether you’re interested in the state’s colonial history or modern attractions, Maryland is more than just crab crakes, Natty Boh, and Old Bay! Here’s just some of the sights and activities you can experience in the Old Line State:
·         Stroll the Ocean City Boardwalk
·         Experience the Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad
·         Treat yourself in Baltimore’s Inner Harbor
·         Enjoy colonial sites such as the Chesapeake Bay Maritime Museum or Ft. McHenry.
·         Watch the Baltimore Ravens or Orioles play live
·         Did we mention the crab cakes, Natty Boh, and Old Bay?
Learn more on the Maryland Tourism official website.
With its  1 rated public school system and beaches, mountains, and tons of green space nearby, Maryland is a great place to live and raise a family. ZeniMax Online Studios is based in Hunt Valley, located north of Baltimore. In this historic community, there are numerous attractions you can enjoy, including the nearby Oregon Ridge Park, golfing, and more.
Learn more about living in Maryland on their official website.
With Washington DC only an hour away, and Philadelphia and New York within easy reach, Baltimore's central location gives residents many options and opportunities for adventure. Whether by plane, train, or automobile, the whole East Coast is yours to be explored!
·         ----------- Subsections -----
·         SUBHEADING: LIFE IN MARYLAND
·         Maryland is not one place, but many places. It provides the perfect balance of Work Life, Family Life & Nightlife. The company didn't happen to sprout in Maryland, we chose Maryland, because it's the best place for our careers and the best place for our families.
·
·         Work life
·         Higher Education
·         Roads / Commutes
·         Surrounded by tech opportunities and partnerships
·         Idea: if it's subtle, maybe we can have people with profiles give a few words on why they like Maryland. Their pic would like back to their profile.

·         Family life:
·         Public schools
·         Parks
·         Museums & Libraries
·         Medical Care
·         Safe property investments
·         Close to the Nation's capitol

·         Nightlife / Culture: This is where we can highlight the charm of Baltimore.
·         Inner harbor
·         Food
·         Sports
·         Events/Festivals: https://www.visitmaryland.org/list/top-maryland-events-and-festivals (This section shows that Maryland is full of energy and a fun place to live) Link out to top ten events.

·         Map: This section could be a full page width map showing driving times of the best places around us. Perhaps we can have some representative ticks speckled on the map to show employee homes. This will show a diverse range of location/commute options.
·         Inner harbor
·         Beach
·         Mountains
·         DC
·         Bethesda Softworks

Q4 Promo Notes from Slack

1) name is TBD, but will be thematically similar to last year's "10 Million Stories"; let's just use that while creating hte page.
2) Goals: Drive DAU, reacquire lapsed players, drive incremental monetization, encourage sharing of positive sentiment in social channels, improve sentiment
3) Assume contest rules are same as last year (this assumes you mean sweepstakes)
4) Sweepstakes will run when event begins with Witches Fest until it ends in December
5) How contest changes - These details are WIP
6) platform specific details, no
7) Sweepstakes prizing - assume similar/same as last year. A suite of physical and/or in-game goods
8) Dungeon event purpose: To drive DAU and engagement with Dungeons, a sticky system in-game. It is cool b/c you get a special event "loot box" that contains a huge suite of awesome in-game items: mounts, pets, houses, super rare stuff.
9) working on examples

completed summary analysis of what's working on the v2 promo page vs what was used for  10MILLIONSTORIES

A couple of areas stand out
- expanding the role and refining the floating nav unit on the right side of the page with the creative direction towards creating a dynamic sidebar
- collapsing non-essential text into accordions, or behind widgets
- providing interactables, such as the character selection control on the Summerset page
	+ expanding the storytelling possibilities here
- encapsulate content inside sections with bkg gfx fill
- use 2 layers of bkg
	+ page bkg
	+ section bkg
- how to show multiple sub-events w timers and description
- what does the page look like when the contest is over?

What is the big deal about the dungeion event? Describe it:

Earn awesome loot by completing random dungeons!

DO THIS
complete a random dungeon via the Dungeon Finder
	You can obtain a Mysterious Reward Box once per day per character.

GET THIS
Earn a Mysterious Reward Box
	This curious cache could contain loads of awesome in-game rewards: consumables, pets, costumes, and even a massive collection of super rare mounts and houses.

WHAT'S NEXT
	DAY 1:
		Festival Bonus
		over
	DAY 2:
		50% more horses!
		23:59 remaining
	DAY 3:
		Home Makeover Special!
		pending
	DAY 5:
		Festival Wrapup Surprise!
		pending

LEARN MORE
On each day of the event between Nov. 30 and Dec. 6 that you complete a random dungeon via the Dungeon Finder, you will also be granted one bonus entry into the ESO  10 Million Stories Sweepstakes, up to a max of seven bonus entries.

Players talking about Dungeon Events
I have been trying to find info on this for some time and i'm sorry if this was asked before and i missed it. but for the purposes of this event. when exactly do dailies reset. like... you can do one random daily per character per day to get a reward box, but when do they reset exactly?

when exactly does each random dungeon daily slot begin and end?

honestly, I would love to know the exact reset times, because scheduling things is tough enough as it is. missing a day, because reset was at some odd early evening time or something would kinda suck.

Twitter:
Can I do the Dungeons even with a low level ? Cause Im like level 22 :/

Can I do the Dungeons even with a low level?

GENERAL APPLICATION
- appears as the default item in a career listing
- Problem is that it presents as a job, and has similar behavior as a job listing in the accordion nav, however it isnt a job. It's less than that, as seen by the absence of any other metadata
- Concern is that the existing metadata (department, division, previously applied CTA) is either extraneous or ambiguous. Attaching it to a catagorized department causes useless friction. (is this a general application for a department? What department should I choose?) Those questions shouldn't arise in the first place
- Goal is to remove the ambiguity and present the general application as unique and seperate from a career listing.

READDRESS CONTENT & LAYOUT FOR GAMES PAGE
- Based on Matt's commentary about "Branching Out. More than Just ESO"
- requires additional image acquisition and curation for page header
- requires multiple game sections
- Discuss with Dom & Eric tomorrow to work out a solution

Oh this is interesting. You can insert media queries in a text field like this.

<style>
@media (min-width: 0px) {
    .override-1 {
        font-size: 12px !important;
    }
}
@media (min-width: 768px) {
    .override-1 {
        font-size: 14px !important;
    }
}
@media (min-width: 992px) {
    .override-1 {
        font-size: 16px !important;
    }
}
@media (min-width: 1200px) {
    .override-1 {
        font-size: 19px !important;
    }
}
</style>
<blockquote class="override-1">Join us in celebrating the amazing community of The Elder Scrolls Online with months of in-game events, prizes, and epic rewards!</blockquote>

<h2 class="editorialheader">New Zone</h2>
<p>Discover the ancient homeland of the High Elves. Encounter shining cities, vibrant forests, tropical lagoons, coral caves, mysterious ruins, and more.</p>

<h2 class="editorialheader">New Necromancer Class</h2>
<p>While others use magic to do paltry things like conjure fire or fly, the Necromancer is a master over death itself. They study the deep and forbidden secrets that raise the dead, controlling minions toward a variety of goals. Perhaps they seek the power that mastery over death provides.</p>

<h2 class="editorialheader">Dragons</h2>
<p>In her book The First Fossil Hunters: Dinosaurs, Mammoths, and Myth in Greek and Roman Times (2000), Adrienne Mayor argues that some stories of dragons may have been inspired by ancient discoveries of fossils belonging to dinosaurs and other prehistoric animals</p>

<h2 class="editorialheader">Combat</h2>
<p>For almost all purposes, there is no relevance to the end of a round or the beginning of a round. A round can be a segment of game time starting with the first character to act and ending with the last, but it usually means a span of time from one round to the same initiative count in the next round. Effects that last a certain number of rounds end just before the same initiative count that they began on.</p>

The “developer experience” bait-and-switch works by appealing to the listener’s parochial interests as developers or managers, claiming supremacy in one category in order to remove others from the conversation. The swap is executed by implying that by making things better for developers, users will eventually benefit equivalently. The unstated agreement is that developers share all of the same goals with the same intensity as end users and even managers. This is not true.

2018-10-02 13:03:20
follow up notes from MESA Design meeting

The TIMELINE section
PRIMARY
The MESA timeline functions as a menu for directing customers to dedicated MESA promo pages throughout the campaign's lifecycle.


for placement on an arbitrary page (TODO:define/inquire about page types for placement use cases) This MESA menu includes clickables that link out to dedicated MESA promo pages throughout the year. The design of this menu incorporates a thematic background asset (TODO:define/inquire about asset types) which can be specified (or overriden) when placed on a page. The arrangement of the clickables should imply progression (linear/radial) The sizing and messaging of the clickables signifies the differences between Chapter/Event/DLC promo The clickables may be shown in a locked or unlocked state.

SECONDARY
The visual theming of the buttons relates to objects, creatures or characters that hold some significance for their place on the timeline

2018-10-04 10:46:41
collating reference material for MESA MENU design


MESA MENU DESIGN SPECIFICATION

The MESA Menu directs customers to unique MESA URL's throughout the campaigns lifecycle. These URL's are assumed to be dedicated Promotional Pages for DLC and Chapter updates

The MESA Menu is a component that is intended for use on the following page types:
	- Chapter Page
	- DLC Page
	- Custom Page built from Chapter Page or DLC Page Template

This component may also have value if used as a sidebar widget on the following page types:
	- Home Page
	- News Page
	- Article Page

This component includes clickables that link out to dedicated MESA content throughout the campaign

The arrangement of the clickables at any viewport width implies progression (linear/radial)

The sizing/theming of the clickables signifies differences between Chapter, DLC or Event Promotion

The clickables may be shown in an unlocked or locked state
	+ The unlocked state may refer to any available URL, including the current page
	+ The locked state isn't navigable

This component incorporates a background asset which may be specified or overriden when placed on a page
