P1 create wireframe "anatomy" of the item carousel from previous sketches
	+ show use cases:
		* size
			* asset is smaller than container
			* asset is same size as container
			* container offset = 0 so assets are side by side
		* number of items on a row is determined by container width
			- show line of many items (small containers)
		* quantities
			- no items
			- 1 item
			- 2 items
			- 3 items
			- 4 items
			- 5 items
		* sequence
			- first item
			- last item
- export Axure layouts as gfx
- first pass confluence doc
- request new proxy assets from David C

CONSTRAINTS
	+   of items in a viewport is determined by container + spacer width
		+ default size of container for MESA is fixed width/height across all views
		+ default size of spacer is 24px across all views
	+ maximum asset width/height is the container width/height
		+ asset width/height may be less than container width/height
		+ assets and text in a container can be aligned relative to the container
	+ carousel is drawn centered to the viewport when number of items * container is less than viewport width
	+ prev/next buttons appear to show carousel overflow past viewport width
	+ carousel is navigable using prev/next arrow buttons
	+ carousel is navigable by dragging with mouse or finger
	+ the text label area can contain am RGBA texture drawn behind the text
	+ Any visible container is selectable

OPEN QUESTIONS
	+ how does clicking prev/next modify the list view?
		- scroll by 1 item?
		- scroll by n items relative to viewport width
	+ how does dragging the carousel modify the list view?
		* at the end of the drag, list snaps to absolute position
		* list is simply draggable by x pixels

ZENIMAXONLINE.COM

VIDEO HEADER MONTAGE FOR LANDING PAGE

Creative Brief
Theme: Studio Visit

Purpose:
This video asset is used on the landing page of the studio site as a visual anchor summarizing the studio environment, as if seen through the eyes of a visitor

Summary
The video serves as a looping animated texture. None of the footage is intended to be exceptional -  its just day to day stuff, a slice of life taken from the beehive of purposeful activity you'll find at ZOS. The focus is not on characters or lifestyles of any one person. Instead - we focus on the studio environment which consists of exterior imagery and people engaged with each other and tools of the trade. To Eric's point about showing game footage - I think its OK to do so, but will match this theme more effectively if the game is shown on a screen in the environemnt as an object of discussion - not as a focal point. This approach rewards heraldry and incidental objects including symbols from the game (dark brotherhood) as well as RPG-themed decorations on desks. Closeups of team-signatures on concept art on the walls is appreciated.Its about belonging, a glimpse on the inside and you're invited.

Visuals
Visuals should be organized into a standard storytelling sequence - starting from a wide exterior shot of the studio and progressively getting closer through a series of medium shots which transition into closeup shots. Clips should dissolve into other clips, so avoid hard cuts or any stylized transitions. The overall feel of this video sequence needs to be smooooth.

Deliverable
30 second video sequence delivered as high quality master. The webteam will perform additional color correction to this master for inclusion on the studio site.

Duration
30 seconds, looping

Examples
Here are 2 similar approaches.

https://www.ea.com/careers
EA's approach to this is what I'd like to match and was the inspiration for this brief. It keeps the focus on the actual content of the page by obscuring the video content with a dynamic CSS overlay

https://careers.blizzard.com/en-us/
Blizzard's approach is similar to what I've requested, although by comparison to EA, it reads as busy and unfocused.

2018-12-18 08:54:47
Notes from conversation w Dom about Community Guides

we currently show monthly articles that collate several community guides by subject. problem is that individual guides lack visibility, and its not possible to collate lists of guides around related topics (unless you were to do so by hand)

We want the ability to spotlight individual guides
- focus on website/author
- need ability to view previous spotlights

Celebrate the community because they create free content that we can leverage for clicks.
- clicks on what?
- what CTA's can be exposed here?
- standard site sidebar?
	+ crown items
	+ announcements?
	+ featured news posts?

We would like to form ongoing relationships w content creators. similar to the idea of The Stream Team.
- provide official ESO media kit for these creators
	+ badges
	+ hirez logos and art assets
- gamify
	+ provide "XP" for repeat contributions?
	+ are repeat contributions enough of a factor for us to consider?

CONTENT TYPES/CATEGORIZATIONS

Core Categories
	Beginner’s Guides
	Advanced Guides
	Builds

Sub Categories
	Dungeons
	Trials
	PvP
	Questing & Exploration
	Combat
	Crafting & Customization

Sub-sub Categories
	(Guide Creator name)
	(Class name)
	(Release name (Update XX, DLC / Chapter name))

2019-01-14 12:40:46
met w Crown Store team about Crown Store 2.0
Lee wasn't at meeting, so nothing definitive discussed, but circling closer to the concerns at hand. Something was mentioned about a previous UI flow for this? No one seemed to knwo much about that though? Did I head it was attached tio the meeting invite?

My takeaway from the 1/14 meeting is to:
- examine the game for opportunities to surface product recommendations to players and sketch what that may look like. The immediate areas in mind are the Crown Store itself and the purchase dialog. Other areas I'll take a look at are the Collections UI and Player Inventory
- examine the Product Announcement dialog that appears on game launch. As I mentioned to Bobby, I'm interested in exposing that same info in the game menu itself. I will also present a sketch of that mechanism
- I will take a look at "how to purchase the recommended things". My first thought on that is a shopping cart in the Crown Store. I can sketch out what that can look like in the form of some flow diagrams

Some new team members:
Tim Schroeder
Sahil Jhangiani
Karl Mueck

2019-01-18 08:14:00
collated meeting notes for Crown STore team
Some quick notes from the last meeting, since I am finally unburied from Mesa and RC Lock:

•	After discussion, it seems we’re more interested in targeting users with recommendations based on global purchase data vs personal purchase data.
o	IE: Players who like X also like Y, instead of you’ve purchased X so you might also want Y.
o	There were some concerns about GDPR implications if we present this as a direct use of personal data instead of anonymous.
•	Per Gary, the most optimal solution is to build a shopping-cart experience
o	The work-abounds, such as allowing players to flag items to purchase later, all build friction into the buying experience
o	We’d have to design the cart in a way that doesn’t also build in friction/extra clicks/confusion
o	With a cart, we can better bundle and recommend items because players will purchase them at once (IE: I have a laptop in my cart and it recommends the cables and software)

Follow-up
•	Gary would like to speak 1 on 1 with BI about the data types we’re able to parse so he doesn’t have to make up categories.
•	Gary is working on very basic flows/material to present in our next meeting.
•	(Complete) Gary requested the previous data BI showed Lee. (This has been sent)
•	Doug/Crown  – Once we know what data we can use, how do we identify undesirable combinations?
o	Pairing items that are already bundled.
o	Recommending items that are not currently for sale. (BI cannot see what is in the inventory currently)
o	Are there any known requirements for where Gary can embed announcements?

2019-01-19 12:37:15
reviewing CSS updates to ZOS site. Overall impression is good.

CAREER LISTINGS
the text we're pulling in has additional cruft that breaks visual consistency, because each job listing applies vertical spacing inside the content. Can we  filter the text? My tests these entries manually (in dev tools) yields the expected visual design
- filter out any empty <p> tags (which are used in the original text to create vertical spacing).
- filter out any <br> tags appearing as the first element inside a <p> tag
- remove any style declarations on imported text
- opening an accordion should close any open accordions in a different section. only one should be open at a time
- Page HEader is inheriting the padding value of career items, causing its margins to be different than page headers on other pages
- increade font sizing of job titles to 16px 700

CONSTANT WHITESPACE BETWEEN SECTIONS
I can demonstrate this to Danny and he'll solve th layout. Overall there's good consistency, but wanting to make sutre its totally uniform, as the non-uniform bits stand out uncomfortably

EMPLOYEE PORTRAITS
- image heights are inconsistent, but why? Are the source images not uniformly sized?
	- I think this is because the min-height is set too low and placed text is causing the height of the container to be variable
- image hover causes image border to expand as well. I want to keep a consistent border, like a window behind which the image expands/contracts

HORIZONTAL RULES
Using the HR tag for horizontal rules causes some layout inconsistency. I like the inset styling seen on the ABOUT page. This is setup using the border proerty of a section. By comparison, the HR tags used to create horizontal rulins on the EMPLOYEE LIST page, dont take on the inset property, and don't allow the same vertical spacing options

RED SECTION HEADERS
These should not be inset on Department pages (the CSS currently insets these by 15px left/right). They need to match styling established on usage of landing page (840px wide at widest view)

PAGE HEADER TEXT
Make sure the H4 tag containing "Zenimax Online Studios" at top of page is consistent on all pages.
baseline: 14px
min width: 768px: 18px
min-width: 992px: 24px

Make H1 containing page title have line-height:1.1 such thjat the leading isn't too tight when the display is multiline

EMPLOYEE PROFILE PAGE
- make sure there's consitent whitespace beneath the page header and the leading paragraph text. The spacing is too tight on EMployee profile pages. margin-top should be 50px
- lineheight of .employee-profile text should be set to 1.8
- icon used for "all departments" in header bloack at bottom of page should be the filled right arrow used elsewhere

2019-02-14 10:36:06
discussed the filtering issue with Will, and we came up with the RIGHT solution. Its so obvious in hindsight actually.
1. The list doesn't show tabs. It shows all live streams by default
2. The language filters affect the live streams listing as expected
3. All Channels is exposed as a filter in the dropdown, and in that way - there is no way to filter the list by language, we are simply showing the entire listing

2019-03-25 15:44:29
KESTREL Priorities
(these were provided to Sprung Studios)
Our Priorities for Sprung:
1.How do we ground a 3rd Person camera with an AR HUD?
2.How is AR represented in the world space?
a.How far do we want to go with AR?
Is it connected to everything?
3.General HUD: Layout, Component Visibility
	a.Component Visual Treatments (Compass, Abilities, Weapons, etc.)
		i.How minimal can we go with the HUD given our core mechanics (Can inactive items be automatically hidden?)
		ii.POTENTIAL RULE: All UI elements are context sensitive and our goal is minimalism and clutter-free presentation
		iii.Is there an approach we can take to the compass bar that’s more suited to our vertical world?
	b.Secondary HUD Treatment (How buttons, interact prompts and instructions are represented on screen)
4.Visual Style Guide for HUD
5.How is AR represented on weapons?
6.Ability Usage
7.Main Menu (Apartment) Treatment *
8.Focus Fire and Ambush Mechanics
9.Side Job Presentation Treatment (Wanted Poster, Courier)
10.Weapons Vendor Advertisement

REQUIRES ADDITIONAL DESIGN DOCUMENTATION
Open Questions:
What can we (ZOS) provide Sprung to help them solve, or at least dig into, those problems? What do we want to have solidified in advance of moving into Full Production?

    padding: 8px;
    text-align: center;
    min-height: 210px;

}

    color:  b8a779;

p.media-type {

}

.price p {
    margin-bottom: 0px !important;
}

 shop-show-product  product-details p {
    margin: 0;
    margin-bottom: 20px;
}

    position: relative;
    min-height: 1px;
    padding-left: 15px;
    padding-right: 15px;

    text-transform: none;

}

    font-size: 35px;
    color:  fff;

}

 shop-show-product  product-details .details-body {
    position: relative;
    float: left;
    width: 66.6666666667%;
    min-height: 1px;
    padding-left: 15px;
    padding-right: 15px;
    margin-bottom: 10px;
}

 shop-show-product  product-details .details-body .details {
    position: relative;
    min-height: 1px;
    padding-left: 15px;
    padding-right: 15px;
    padding-top: 10px;
    padding-left: 0px;
    clear: both;
    font-size: 14px;
    line-height: 1.6;
    word-wrap: break-word;
}

 shop-show-product  product-details .details-body .details strong {
    font-size: 14px;
    text-transform: uppercase;
}

@media (min-width: 992px) {
     shop-show-product  product-details .details-body .controls .btn {
        width:auto;
        padding: 6px 28px
    }
}

2019-04-14 20:21:04
I've been collating my Kestrel notes from last week's trip into a Mindmap

NEXT
respond to Ben's high level questions:

WHAT IS THE ONE THING WE MUST GET RIGHT TO MAKE THIS GAME’S UX/UI WORTH UNDERTAKING?
Formally, we must understand the role of multiple presentation layers in the world and the front end, and must know when, why and how to exploit these patterns fluidly. Put another way, we need to understand where the Fourth Wall begins and ends.

Visually, we must consider the entire environment to be "alive" in some sense. We're all familiar with the idea of the Smart Home, but this is a Smart City. This bookends nicely the idea of an all-seeing AI behind the scenes, but is also becoming the reality for personal and public spaces. Understanding how smart objects behave in public is something which is increasingly important in our lives.

Thematically, As Jared mentioned, we need to approach our Futurism with an understanding of where the technology came from and the reasons it exists.The most interesting thing to me about this project is the feedback loops existing between the real world and the game world. It is conceptually fascinating to think about Augmented Reality in the third person.

Strategically, UX/UI is worth undertaking because it is literally the boundary between customers and a product. As I mention below, the definition of "customer" is fairly elastic, ranging from paying customer, to passive viewer, to journalist to customer support, and so on.


HOW DO YOU ALL DEFINE SUCCESS IN UX? HOW ARE WE BREAKING DOWN THE AREAS OF RESPONSIBILITY IN ORDER TO ACHIEVE THIS SUCCESS?

I love when creative work gets to a place where it "writes itself". For this project, I define overall success as constructing a vision that is easy to communicate to the public and our peers. Commercial success to me looks like acknowledgement from the market and well as making a personal connection with consumers. I would love to see our UX/UI inspire this industry in the way that Dead Space and Horizon Zero Dawn captured my imagination.

Empathy, Scalability, Accountability and Serendipity are the criteria by which I measure success. Good UX doesn't try to be all things to all people - rather, it has a point of view. That point of view emerges as a synthesis of each of these criteria. As creators see the product differently than our customers, and differently than a journalist or someone in community support.

Similarly, part of Good UX is identifying patterns through research and observation. At this early stage, the onboarding materials, games designs and prototyped behaviors we examined are a huge step forward for ZOS in presenting a coherent and inspiring vision to a particular group of "users" - content creators and developers. One challenge ahead of us will be to broaden this footprint.




WHAT ASPECTS OF THE INTERNAL CULTURE OR EXTERNAL ENVIRONMENT COULD PUT THIS GAME’S UX/UI AT RISK TO FAIL?
Watching Dave work through game mechanics using the ESO engine seemed very practical to me, but also suspicious. This methodology raised red flags for me, having used it before as a designer. I wanted to take what he had done and add to it. For me, the ESO system is a closed box which is hard to open. For a UI designer (or any artist) to draw a rectangle onscreen (for example) takes multiple people and a pipeline which is overkill for that designer's needs and purposes. However the new rendering system may be months or years away. Investing time in ESO-style development may be a sunk cost that takes control, ownership, iteration and expression out of the hands of designers.

I feel that the switch to an Unreal-based work environment needs to be prioritized. ZOS has become very familiar with the features and limitations of an existing systems but I do not feel that using this system until a finalized new system comes along is the right way to "Think Different" or think long-term. It is important for UX/UI people (and other creators)  to "get skin in the game" so we can better work with our Art, Design, Engineering and Animation teammates.

I keep asking myself, is reliance on Confluence for capturing documentation problematic? Unless documentation is created in Confluence itself, embedded links do not remain live, and worse - are not searchable. But I really like the kind of documentation I'm seeing the team produce. Its great to see such a strong emphasis made on presentation and graphics. I can't help looking at alternative solutions such as Google's ecosystem of Docs, Sheets, Slides and so forth, which are broadly used for collaborative document creation, sharing and presentation. Just a thought. I am sure we will work this out.




ASSUMING WE MITIGATE THAT RISK, WHAT WOULD EXCEED YOUR WILDEST DREAMS?
Designing with the delivery system offers context and control to a broader audience and a broader group of creators. There are of course other design processes and artifacts. Interactive visualization capability is just a tool. But what an amazing tool! As a designer and developer, I've found great value in mobile dev and webdev processes in which the authoring environments are largely the same as the delivery environment, leading to faster experimentation, easy sharing, more iteration and quicker course correction.

My hope is that UX/UI can help inform and inspire the design effort and identify behavioral and presentational issues early on. Just once, I would love to avoid overdesigning :)

Its not so much a matter of getting it right or wrong, but rather getting to next steps. Traversal, targeting and ability usage are a couple areas which rely on stacking behaviors from different systems. There is nothing like seeing this all happening at once in context; being able to "touch" the design as a whole. At its best, UX/UI shapes the perception of a product - how it is experienced. How I would love to be able to work directly with a game designer or engineer - to build on their work even as they build on my own - to realize and perhaps even inspire their thinking about their own work

2019-05-09 13:30:59
the presentation yesterday was productive, and raised some intderesting questions and pointed out some a priori assumptions which may be reasonable to pursue of abandon, depending on how this designe evolves.

I'm trying to emphasize a non-modal and immersive behavior type in which looking around the physical environment is the same as scanning it in cyberspace. Now it may be that those two activities don't overlap - whether for behavioral or narrative reasons. Its uncertain.

HZD has been my inspiration recently, and one of its strengths is how the scanning cyberspace (second sight) and observing the world are different activities. There are numerous cases where something is only achievable in the real world vs cyberspace, and vice versa. There are also some cool overlaps when looking at the real world through second sight feels like a super power.

Only more questions.

At the moment, I'm going through the screen captures and video made earlier so I can gain better insights on what I think might be termed Phases of a quest. I want to be able to identify these clearly.

New Side Job
Missing Person

Name:	Janov Pelorat
Age:	99
Height:	7'10'
Bio:	Janov Pelorat is a character in the Foundation Series of books by Isaac Asimov. The two books in which he appears are Foundation's Edge and Foundation and Earth. Pelorat is a professor of ancient history who has spent his entire life on his obsession of finding Earth, the mythical planet of human origin. He has a particular interest in myths and folklore. Janov's interest in Earth began when, at age fifteen during some indisposition, he was given a book of legends about the origin of humanity. On Terminus, Janov has collected a massive amount of data regarding the Origin Question, and is Terminus' foremost expert regarding Earth. Janov Pelorat first appears in Foundation's Edge. He is described as white-haired, of average height and weight, and moves without haste and speaks with deliberation. He is 52 years of age but appears considerably older. He has been married in the past, and has a housekeeper named Kloda.(1)

Unusual for a historian of the futuristic society he belongs to, at the time he is first mentioned, he has never left the planet Terminus. Therefore, in addition to the resources available to him from the Terminus University Library, he makes use of interlibrary loans. Such loans can make use of "hyper-radiational signals", which are not described in the stories but appear to refer to a method of transmitting information over vast distances at speeds faster than light.(1)

2019-05-28 09:04:23
we'll be planning the sprint at the Wednesday meeting so any presentation needs to be abbreviated, and I think the idea of sharing the ongoing work in an HTML deployment is a great idea, which speaks to the approach I like to take of making gains in foundational areas. There is often a "chicken and egg" aspect to the order of operations when working on a project at a foundational level. I can easily get myself in a weird loop thinking about it that way and prefer to take an approach in which answers questions experientally and procedurally.

I think its important to summarize what I've l;earned from the current design exercise
- How to lay out UI elements with a consitent scale and desity in a 3 D environment
- Went from knowing nothing about Unreal to feeling somewhat comfortable using the tools as a sketchbook
- Found a way to present design work so you can experience it using a game controller
- Studied and implemented non-modal interactions with an AR environment projected into Worldspace like a hologram
- Refined Player interactions for simple interactions with a Side Job
- Discovered that AR has a voice and demonstrated relationships between:
	- the Public voice (what the public sees)
	- PAN's voices
		- The Application Layer (Revenant utilities and privileged info)
		- The System layer (Visible Grid, System status)
In general, the Public Face of AR is noisy. All available space can be used for advertising. something like a "Missing Person" poster can easily be lost in the noise. Approaching these objectives as a revenant provides a clear and actionable view of the mission and the player gets access to PAN's archives (the real story)
- Conceptualized The Boundaries of the Interface - a scheme for understanding where information can be represented and why
- I used this work as the basis for my AR framework design

SPRINT 11 FOR THE CONTENT TEAM HAS BEEN ACTIVATED:

https://jira.zenimax.com/secure/RapidBoard.jspa?rapidView=1011&selectedIssue=KES-1522

Please take a moment to review your scheduled work and get back to me if you spot any errors or omissions. As a reminder – the sprint ends Tuesday, June 25th to allow stakeholders to review submitted work June 26th and 27th before the Sprint review on the 28th.

The top-level goals for the sprint are:

•	Complete a working version of the final Presentation for the June update to Rockville
•	Get a first pass at MSFT Teams implemented as a working and useful tool for the team
•	Provide an updated version of the project roadmap: deliverables and goals checklist for each development phase, and a clearer understanding of overall dev process
•	Gameplay Prototyping:
o	Support Rockville Presentation Gameplay
	Build gameplay to support the Rockville Presentation:
	Capture and playable demo space
	Refine traversal mechanics for Rockville Presentation
o	Art support for the presentation with concept art, 3d key art, and environment production
•	Solidify design structure for Quests and Side Jobs
•	Solidify the next pass at our Main Story, incorporating all valid feedback
•	Engine and Tools – TBD

2019-06-22 11:30:06
Doing some research and collation to understand Interactable objective patterns. The consensus arrived at during last week's presentation was that non-modal interactions and presentation of UI will not scale well and become increasingly elaborate.

The challengee is to retain spatial awareness during UI interactions. "Keep the player movin" as David said.

Jared expressed support for the visualization work that I'm doing and wants to see more of it. He specifically mentioned a camera transition to frame a UI surface, rather than trying to literally manipulate the world space projections (my words). Without specifically asking, its clear that's what he;d like to see - something resembling The Division. I asked this directly to the group, and that seemed to be the general feeling. Perhaps misguided, as many of those guys play The Division regularly. But the game solves some of these same issues and the ideas there can definitley be built upon, if only as a source of status quo/truth

I've done extensive research on Watchdogs2. Maybe too much given the goals I've set for this presentations. I felt like I learned a lot from this game however. The distinction between my visible grid concept and AR-Assist for example. And also a good example of using a keybinds in conjunction with a contextual interact menu. Finally, the value and inevitability of interaction at a distance. This makes the conceit of these objectives being hosted on a network much more plausible.

KESTREL build intsallation instructions
Note that you want to go to:
https://confluence.zenimaxonline.com/display/KES/Getting+the+Prototype
for instruction on install.
When you install:
Note you will need a controller, and make sure you get all of the correct bindings.
The picture shows 3 binding changes but don’t skip the steps:
•
•	B → Boost
•	HOLD X → Interact/Drop (this will trigger Grapple Release, and Drop from Jut and Ledges)
•	The background of the button will turn white w/black text.
•	LT → Aim
•	Additional changes not in image:
•	Toggle Mount: Change R to Left Bumper(LB) → Grapple
•	Weapon Swap: Bind to PRESS X for Reload.
•	Weapon Selector Advance: Bind to Right Stick Press

2019-07-15 07:26:10
Roy sent me an interesting reference for a spatial UI called "Solar Wheels". Its a cool idea and very much inline with progressive disclosure on approach. It intrioduces something I hadn't really thought about too much - how information transforms on approach. You aren't seeing more - you're drilling in and seeing less in greater detail.
https://pdfs.semanticscholar.org/1f94/6c109c6d86a7b4183b00d23a096670175f05.pdf
https://vimeo.com/72357002

I also like the presentation style. Its just simple motion graphics but conveys intent well and concisely without too much effort on the production value or realism of the UI

2019-07-18 09:47:50
I captured feedback in detail during the presentation and Roy was kind enough to provide a detailed writeup on Slack, copied here:

	Roy McCormack (05:38)
	I took a few notes in yesterday's meeting that I didn't get a chance to cover. As always there was a lot of good stuff presented.

	First, regarding Keyan's UI mockups:
	I like the idea of there being lanes on which information flows in, It feels like a good way to reduce the work needed on the player's part to center attention on the new info, process it quickly and refocus on their prior goal or find a new one.

	I agree with Gary that thinking of each component of the screen space UI as its own element could yield good benefits. For one I like the potential to connect each element to something in world space in a way that doesn't look clunky.

	Couple of notes on Anton's weapon customization screens:

	I was wondering if L1 and R1 might not be permitted to context switch what weapon you are looking at once you've dug a layer or two deep into the mod menus?

	Re. auto-select based on parameters:
	One thing to note is that some weapon types may change quite drastically based on mods in very different ways than other weapon types. This complicates any kind of auto-selection helper system quite a bit.
	It is also worth considering that we are looking at lower enemy counts with deeper enemy variety where individual enemies present more unique challenges.

	It made me think of Warframe. They do have an auto-mod-select system but it is terrible. At this point in that game's lifecycle there must be one hundred mod types for each weapon type. Their original solution was the most functional which was to simply include the concept of mod loadouts per weapon. All weapons come with three.

	Re. Gary's mockups:
	I really liked the addition of making the bounds for interactions visible in a subtle way. It made me wonder what other messaging (audio, fx notifiers, animation states etc...) would be there to inform a player that they've entered an AR interactionn bounds.

	The monocle filigree made me wonder if the elements of the filigree could be themed to specific types of interactions that are possible with the monocle. Imagine that those elements react when you pass the monocle over something that they are associated with to help players get a quick read on the nature of the interaction. Just a thought

2019-07-29 07:16:07
SPRINT GOALS

MOVE FROM BEHAVIORS TO SCENARIOS
At Jared's suggestion, I want to broaden the scope of my ongoing PAN/OS visualizations to enact specific scenarios. I've previously examined several mechanical designs and want to use that material to communicate simple stories.

Examples:
- Open the locked door
- Follow the trail
- Find the hidden object
- Gated objective

I will consult with a writer (TBD) to develop a pool of simple, relevant scenarios and stage them in an interactive visualization to refine and extend my previous spatial interface design work.

VISUALIZE THE HUD
I want to work with Keyan to build out a layered HUD represesentation incorporating motion and transitional behaviors. This work is non-interactive and intends to provide tangible feedback of spatial relationships to help inform the visual design and art direction of the HUD presence.

Preparing material for presentation later

Here is a Windows build of the visualization I'm presenting later.
\\hvault\Projects\Kestrel\UI\SKINJESTER\PAN0820\Demo_PAN0820.zip

I've only tested on my workstation but will most likely run on yours without incident.

CONTROLS
Move around with WASD keys and mouse, or use a gamepad.
Skip cinematics: X key or DPAD-UP
Toggle Fullscreen: F11
Exit: ALT-F4

BUGS
I noticed a couple quirky edge cases. Hopefully you won't, but if you find yourself in an odd state, press X (or DPAD-U) until you aren't anymore.


CHANGELIST:
integrated media from ZOS storyboard movie as cutscenes
added spatial triggers for media sequences
added option to skip cutscenes
built out scenario 0101: Identity Scan
player character starts as Trey, becomes female protagonist after character select
added Calder & The Mayor to crime scene

2019-08-26 09:39:46
Peparing for sprint 14 planning meeting.
Reviewing and clarifying my workload

I'd like to segment my workload into 2 different stories:
Create and explore via Unreal engine a complete UI Taxonomy of HUD, Object/NPC Interactions, Clues, Conversations.  What would the in-game UI/UX flow appear for an actual use-case for accepting and starting a Case?

UX flow and UI wireframes for starting a case by analyzing clues at a crime scene to turn them into actionable leads

Content matrix for HUD, Object/NPC interactions, Clues and Markers

2019-08-27 07:56:07
collating notes for brief discussion w Dave & Wynne

(1) Incidents can be investigated on-site in real time
(2) Incidents (along with any discoveries/outcomes) can be reviewed and investigated from a backlog of case files at any time
(3) Any discoveries/outcomes of an investigation are stored to a backlog of case files

Given these principles,ARIS may be represented by the following views:

- A navigable spatial representation of the Drone's incident scan, which
    + shows marked clues, selectable for investigation
    + shows AR-readout (profile) for a selected clue
    + shows interact prompt for analysis
    + shows interact prompt for research
    + updates clue state to a case file archive

- A case file browser, which
	+ is accessible from the scanning mode
	+ is accessible from a pause menu
	+ allows review of all collected incidents
	+ always invokes a spatial representation of the selected incident (from the Drone's memory)
- Investigation notes, which
	+ Enumerates investigated clues
	+ Enumerates analysis results
	+ Enumerates research results
- To-Do list
	+ selectable list of actions derived from the investigation

Design work can roughly be divided into three stages: early conceptual work, mid-stage mock and prototype creation, and late-stage build. I setup a phone chat w Jason about segmented meter layout.
The issue I'm having with it is that if shown as a line - it starts small (you only have 3 segments to your health pool at game start), but as gameplay progresses, additional segments are added. The challenge is how to keep the display contained but also visible peripherally and at a glance

I have a vague idea in mind of using a segmented radial meter for player health in the lower left, more of an arc, that becomes increasingly circular as segments are added

2020-01-06 19:08:52

Amps are abilities that take physical form, ranging from weaponized cybernetics to wearable gear. Each Amp provides a bit of functionality that its upgrades improve and expand upon.

They feed into different player character fantasies by offering RPG class-like functions that typically come from skill-tree style progression. They come in a variety of forms from weaponized cybernetics to wearable gear. Players will also acquire mods for Amps that improve and expand on their functions

2020-01-06 19:30:02
I've reviewed a number of design docs, including the Health and Energy docs. From that I created a list of terms and defined each of them. I'm glad I did. AMPS aren't what i thought they were at all. They're like magical items, not abilities. Skills (abilities) are also a thing, athough the documentation doesn't mention what they cost.

I think its safe to assume they can be used at any time but have a cooldown.
How are they triggered though?
Do I queue up selected skills in an ability belt?
Do I queue up AMPS in an ability belt?
Do I navigate through an ability belt?

Early HUD mockups by David show something like a Player Ability belt
I think it's OK to treat Skills like this.
They could also be pre-assigned to keybinds

Can the same system be scaled to use AMPS though? Maybe, but that is a potentially long list. 7 items in HZD feels a bit long.

How are the Skills in The Division used?
- I think they can be used at any time via a keybind and have an associated cooldown?
-
How are the Skills in Horizon Zero Dawn used?
- the HZD skills don't cost anything to use. Many of them are situational, like a sneak attack while hiding. Some have a duration and cooldown - such as advanced aiming

2020-01-06 19:52:22
The design documentation spells it out:
- Skills add depth to existing combat functions
- Skills upgrade the effect of existing inputs or create new context-based inputs

In the visual examples provided, the Skill is activated in a particular situation and prompted by a keybind at the appropriate time. Captured that in my Gameplay Moments dataset



2020-01-06 20:08:49
The player may only equip one Amp at a time

Amps are selected as part of the player’s loadout and may be changed whenever there is access to the loadout.

Amps must be primed (or readied) before use with X + SQUARE

Once readied, Amps can be activated with RT

Amps use an Energy Bar to limit how often and for how long the player can use them.
- Different Amps use the Energy Bar in different ways.
	- The Threat Scan Amp, which shows enemy silhouettes through geometry, will imediately consume an amount of energy per activation
	- The Energy Shield Amp will drain energy at a defined rate for as long as the shield is active.

Skills do not drain the energy bar.

2020-01-06 21:38:58
I finished identifying and defining all the terms in the design documentation. Pretty illuminating. The HUD representation of Amps and Skills will be much simpler than expected. Not shown as a list but as an opportunity or triggered action.

2020-01-28 13:43:30
Hi everyone,
Gary’s working on the UX and UI for Interact Point Attractors this sprint. To get him started, I wanted to throw a few use cases his way to get him started. Here are the ones I can think of right now.



- Speak to a character
- Open a container
- Pick up a Resource
	Resources are broken down into materials the player will give to armorers and gunsmiths to turn into equipment. This whole process still needs design.
- Interface with a computer system
	This isn’t a “terminal,” strictly speaking, but a designated spot in gameplay where the player engages with a feature
	Examples:
		Customize their drone, apartment, armor, weapons, etc.
		Browse a store’s catalog
		Access their Feed
			(we were calling this the Mission Terminal earlier – it’s an access point in their apartment where we show them relevant content for the progress in the story, contracts, stuff for sale, etc.
- Engage with a Fast Travel station
- Access “Lore” – we don’t know what these are yet exactly, but they work the same way Skyrim’s books do: They provide some back story and lore about a part or aspect of the world
- Pick up and carry an object. This is for objects too large to fit in their inventory – the player will need to use both hands here. These objects haven’t been designed yet.
- Enter a vehicle (or hop onto a mount like a Tauntaun or something). This hasn’t really been designed yet either.
- Find a vendor
- Open a door
- Pickup Loot
- Pickup ammo
- Pickup Health consumable

2020-01-28 14:25:23
updated presentation template for data capture. What are the points to go over with Jason ?

Context
Manage expectations - discuss what I expect to present tomorrow
- use cases categorized by visual posture and maybe other criteria?

What would Jason like to see?
- rules for display and engagement

Ideation
When there's some experience to be had "inside" the interactable (a UI mode, a list, a dashboard, a content viewer), the camera takes on a life of its own and pushes forward to frame the UI

Auto pickup vs Intentional pickup
What if I'm sprinting or running by
- Some interactables (consumables, ammo) pickup automatically when you pass by
- Some interactables present a prompt for further usage
- Some interactables respond to proximity as a trigger (an NPC or vendor profile, for example)

Interaction marker parameters include:
- What triggers visible/hidden state
- Secondary animations as attract loops
- Geometry state (outlined, highlighted, hidden (?) )
- World state (visible grid, paths, decals)
- Marker posture (camera facing, world orientation, placed on screen, placed in world, distance scaling)
- Narrative conceit (AR tag, Hologram)
- Camera behavior (pushes in to look closer when entering trigger volume, reframes when player opens UI)
- Visible on compass?
-

How do I know when I can interact with something in the game?
It shows a marker
It shows a label
It is highlighted
It is outlined
Something else points at it
The camera frames it
The environment around it emphasizes it

How can I interact with entities in the game?
- Target it
- Shoot at it
- Approach it
- Wait for it after it signals
- Take action when prompted
	- Equip it
	- Hold it
	- Inspect it
	- Collect it
	- Purchase it
	- Speak to it
	- Open a gameplay feature

2020-02-18 08:16:05
reviewing my notes

what is this design?
It is an equipment dashboard used to (1) compare equipment I find with what I have, (2) take action on the equipment I find, for example - taking it or equipping it

The form I want it to take is an in-world interface rather than a front end screen. This isn't the inventory screen

When I find an item, this UI is how I inspect and evaluate it
I only see what is relevant for the thing I found
I only see what is actionable for the thing I found


Defining Relevance
Flow
Wireframe(s)
In-World Framing

2020-02-19 08:07:05
I'm at a place where I can start assembling wireframes from my parts.
The part I haven't figured completely is what content to show for roles. My initial assumptions are naive but will hopefully capture the idea. WHich is what?

The idea is that:
Not all weapons are great but they all serve a purpose.
When comparing a weapon to another, we see an overall rating, and role-based ratings that assess the relative fitness for heavy/assault/sniper usage

the role based ratings only show criteria relative to the fitness assessment.
For example, instead of the full weapon itemization, a sniper-role may only show:
stats: range, damage
mods: any mods affecting range or damage
properties: any properties relevant to range, accuracy, targeting

2020-04-22 19:45
meeting on Friday to discuss Amps. I plan to update the presentation to incorporate the main feedback and also examine the idea of showing the "cybernetics" items as Jared mentioned. I finally understood what he meant when he asked if this was an equipment or inventory screen. The cybernetic parts are in my inventory too. In the context of thios design they are independent slots. Instead of there being a single AMP category, there are "Cybernetic Arm", "Cybernetic Leg", "Psionic" categories. The root level category - "the slot" isn't an empty container - it is the item itself. As far as what actions can be performed upon the Cybernetic Arm on its own - maybe CUSTOMIZE, DISCARD, UPDATE,



2020-04-24 09:02
creating schematics of the design for discussion w David, Roy, Jason
v1 - current. All Amps are listed under a single AMP category. Not considering any manipulations on the actual platform the arm is attached to other than as categorization labels

v2 - unique AMP platforms are listed. Some have AMPS equipped, some do not. Basic CTA for the AMP platforms are OPEN/CUSTOMIZE

ARMOR
	- same pattern as weapons

MODS TAB
Have been focusing exclusively on equipment. This grouping is intended to list mods as primary. I'd sketched out an idea for this previously. The idea is that you choose mods from a list or grid. You get a list of items that the mod can be attached to. On that item, you see the existing mod attachment. You can:
- replace existing Mod
- remove MOD
- add new MOD

Design review yesterday was OK. Some good suggestions were added which I notes and started working on.

The larger issues discussed were
- maintaining connection between interactables in one column when scrolling horizontally into the next.
	+ some of this is an artifact of static wireframes, but even at this level the expected behavior could be telegraphed better
- can you reconsider the layout so that Equipment properties don't appear orphaned
- Include Player Character on left side of screen as a target for the Loadout Tab
- Better definition of Loadout Tab
	+ think more about what can be done here
- Define Mods tab
	+ go back to prior sketches and ideas about this and build out in the design


2020-05-07 16:45
From Max's notes during design review

Went over some work with Jason last week
Came away with discussions with Jared about how we can incorporate the cybernetics to get it represented somehow

- Looked at the idea of treating cybernetics on treating them as "dynamic slots" which didn't quite work
- Ended up modifying the layout to support a more central/hero based focus - taking some cues from skyrim or dragon age
	- In that both of those are less about slots and more about the items themselves

Touring the newest designs (see most recent documentation on hvault)
	- Includes a player status block
	- Features two weapon slots, an amp slot, and four armor slots
	- Treating everything as though they are similar objects - setting some conventions and running with them.
	- Cognizant of the fact that players like seeing the items on their character - which you can currently only do from the loadouts screen - considering if we want to incorporate it into the lower screens somehow

- Explored what it looks like if you have different amps running on the same platform (e.g.- your cybernetic arm can run either armstrong assault or scout drone, for example)

- Showed dedicated "comparison of items" modal screen

FEEDBACK
Landing page wound up in a great spot
- The properties you boiled down to are successful
- Dave wants to spend a little bit of time thinking about the flow for viewing and changing mods - though the parity of how the mods/abilities/etc is successful
- Concerns of whether or not there's enough of a connection between the equipment list and the mod list (particularly for the low items)
	- We could potentially solve this via a fixed focal point
	- Or use a dedicated "configure" launch point
	- This process might be a bit shaky
	- This gap probably won't be as much of an issue for armor, since there aren't customizable elements of armor

- It would be useful to highlight the relevant ammo type when you're focusing on specific weapons
	- Perhaps take it one step further and incorporate the ammo count somewhere more prominently?

- It would indeed be nice to get the player character on the left side of the screen - in the open space?
	- That would work well, from the standpoint of reinforcing that the loadout is to the left
	- Plus we could pan over to the character we selecting loadout

Looking good!
- The standardization is really successful
- Mods will be much more common than weapons as drops
- A future pass of this screen (not this round) will look at cosmetics.
- We'll need to have a meeting about this - determining what the player can do from the field vs. what they need to do to a vendor to take care of.

2020-06-05 11:14
Jason's notes

Story Quests, Missions and Side Jobs can be tracked.

Lowercase quest here means Story Quests, Missions or Side Jobs.

Tracked means always show on my Compass.

Untracked quest markers look different on the compass to set them apart from currently Tracked quests.

Untracking a Tracked quest, changes that quest’s Compass behavior.

Untracked quests are in my Compass when I am in the same area as the quest.

This can mean proximity or generically district or region.
This behavior will need to be configurable by Design.
All quests, Tracked or Untracked, get an icon on the Map.

Quests stay in your Quest Tracker until they are completed.

Completed quests move to that quest’s completed section in the Quest Tracker.

Players can have multiple quests Tracked.

If this causes the compass to become cluttered the player can always choose to Untrack something.
Focusing a quest in your compass will allow players to Track or Untrack that quest by holding the PAN-Link Button.

If we limit the amount of quests the player can track, we will need to revisit this.
Quest information in the HUD will only display when:

The player has highlighted a quest in their compass
The player has selected to Track a quest
The player has done something to update the quest objective
The player activates PAN-Assist
After quest information has been displayed for X seconds on the HUD it will fade out.

Players will be able to change this in settings to have the HUD always display the current quest the player is engaged in.

We will only want to maintain one quest and objective in the HUD in this case.
We will need to define the rules on how this works.
We do not want a Fallout 76 text wall situation, even if the player is opting in


Story Quests will be relatively short and “self-contained”
hours versus days/weeks
Generally focused in one area without traveling vast distances.
The Story Quest might complete and then send you a vast distance to start the next Story Quest in the story chain.
Players might be able to leave a Story Quest in progress and return later, but it will be less common behavior and not the intention of design.


Side Jobs are shorter than Story Quests
There could be multiple Side Jobs in a Side Job chain
Side Jobs could be more than likely in progress while doing other quests


Missions are instanced and short
About 20 – 30 minutes
Instanced means very self-contained
Quest Tracker will direct players to the start of the Mission
Missions can not be left half done or in progress


Player generated quests are variable in length and scope
It might take a player a while to collect enough of a resource to complete a project they are tracking
There is a high probability these quests will be in progress while the player Tracks other Story Quests, Missions or Side Jobs

We will probably have very few Story Quests available at a time since they are all chained.

Missions will be rotating in and out of the system with a maximum amount available at a time.

Side Jobs are finite but collectable. We cannot prevent the player from collecting all of them and never starting them.

Player generated quests, like collecting a specific resource, could be infinite and we have the least control of these.

spent time yesterday stepping through quests in Elder Scrolls Online. Upon returning to an older character I was faced with the matter of not knowing where I'd left off.

The assumption is that where I left off == my current objective

This is not the case in practice. Literally - yes, that current objective is the last thing I was doing, but why was I doing it? What led me to this moment? Did I meet anyone? Where have I been?

2020-06-22 07:58
I've solved the categorization with 2 views: LOCATION & PROGRESS
The page uses HMENU to filter quests by quest type: STORY, SIDE, MISSIONS, ERRANDS

The LOCATION view groups quest content (open or closed) by location. Location containing current objective is shown first. Remaining locations are listed alphabetically. In this view, I can easily see and react to what's around me

The PROGRESS view groups quest content by completion: OPEN, REPLAY, CLOSED. The current objective is shown first. In this view I can easily see and react to how I've invested my time

2020-06-22 13:06
some design specifications for the newly titled HUD-OBJECTIVE

HUD Objective Widget

Goal: As the Player, I want to be able to quickly see what my current objectives are for my tracked quests

Design:

In-world HUD is kept clean and uncluttered by default
HUD Objective Widget is displayed when ( button ) is pressed (PAN-LINK)
The tracked quests are displayed for a brief time before fading out

HUD Objective Widget displays Tracked Quest information when:

- The Player presses  ( button ) PAN-LINK
- Tracked Quest's objective is updated
- Any Quest's objective is updated
- Option to track a New Quest is presented
- Quest is completed (?)
- Transition back to open world from instance (?)
- Player resumes game after quitting (?)

Reference:
- Spider-man
- Tomb Raider


Picking Up New Quests While Tracking Others

Goal: As the Player, I can easily switch between quest content that I wish to pursue

Design:

- HUD Objective Widget gives option to track new quest in in-world HUD when it is discovered
    - Player can Opt In with ( button ) to set as tracked
    - Player can hold ( button ) to jump into Quest Log for more details
    - Play can ignore and UI goes away in a few seconds
    - If not opted to track, can be found in Quest Log
    - The HUD Objective Widget is displayed alongside the prompt for the Player to decide if they wish to change quest tracking (?)


Tracking Multiple Quests

Goal: As the Player, I need to be able to track multiple quests at the same time

Design:
- In Quest Log, Player can select up to ( 5 ) quests to track
- Tracked Quests can be located:
    - Map
    - In Pan-Link menu
    - Quest Log
    - HUD Objective Widget objective list
    - In-world at specific location for objective
    - Tracked quests have unique icon or icon color to differentiate from other content types not tracked (?)

2020-07-01 11:44
My mentor directed me to a Secret Weapon, which turned out to be a bigger deal than he knew. Led me Uptown where trouble had already arrived. Before heading there I reviewed my inventory and found a mod blueprint requiring component X to fabricate. I made a note of it and tracked the location PAN suggested.

I arrived uptown. The Mayor was missing. Found him, and found myself with new obligations. Part of the job. A signal relay went offline as I happened by. Stolen batteries. Stupid. I found the thief and replaced them.

Meanwhile the situation Uptown was getting weird. The Mayor had misplaced a star. An actual star. How...? I followed the story to the Design District. I was feeling anxious so I ran a Maintenance mission before getting back to work.I only hoped I wasn't too late.

PAN signaled me. She'd intercepted a vague transmission near my current position. I'm the only one around. The signal relay wasn't an isolated case. I began monitoring the situation. Now what about that missing star...

2020-07-10 12:27
Don't get bogged down by details. Don't overdesign. DO call out possibilities & facts.

What is the idea?
NAV-ASSIST is a FAST way to look at the map without having to enter a full screen menu. The NAV assisted view is intended to show waypoints. Those waypoints include a subset of the representations I've identified. By centering objectives in view, further information about the target is revealed. Some interactions with the target may be possible from a distance.

Waypoints include behaviors and display states in-world based on proximity and camera point of view.

The next step is to include information specific to Quests

2020-07-14 14:41
finished defining HUD-OBJECTIVE. A better name for it would be HUD-READOUT. Connects naturally to ENTERING NAV-ASSIST. Thinking about that as astatic presence, as a globally relevant "current/last-updated" is a hard habit to break. HUD-READOUT has a couple of different interaction modes. These are described further in my QUEST design.

The mode I'm interested in for NAV-ASSIST treats HUD-READOUT like a "tooltip" for any WAYPOINT centered in the camera view. Compare this with the behavior when NAV-ASSIST is inactive. When in that case, HUD-READOUT behaves like a
"tooltip" for any WAYPOINT centered in the COMPASS view.

2020-09-29 07:58
just wondering if the idea of "Equipment Slots" truly stands up? each of those "slots" could be a simplified equipment list entry, as seen in the EQUIP design

That wouldn't change the spreadsheet listing of inventory items, but it would possibly reduce visual density, and ddefinitely solve some of the issues of representation that have been bothering me. FUndamentally, I don't like it when information is repeated in different context areas. That might be philosophical?

For example, its feels questionable to me that equipment is represented visually in an equipment slot AND seen as a large preview. WHy not just make the preview part of the list? AI'd experimented with that idea and felt I was on to something.

Its important though not to fixate. My goal is to build out a full system as previously concieved. Make it work as a prototype and share that to understand issues beneath the surface that I haven't even considered.

2020-10-27 07:35
updated my weapon content spreadsheet with the new Mods
I want to go back and refine the treatment of Mod values and comparisons. David had mentioned two "channels" for this information. And the idea is captured in the spreassheet as well.

Channel 1:
communicates the magnitude of increase or decrease
+++	Greatly increases/decreases
++	Slightly increases/decreases
+	increases/decreases

Channel 2:
communicates the delta between current and new value
This is what I've continued to communicate with the metering system. By its nature it gets detailed, so it is for advanced customers - min/maxers

good meeting that hilighted what the guys at ZOS are prioritizing
its all about the MODS
that's where I need to be placing my attention

specificially
COMPARING MODS
EQUIPPING MODS.

We came up with some interesting use cases:

I want to see cumulative effect of all equipped MODS

2020-11-14 10:16
updating Equipment List to seperate funnction of Slot gfx and slot label for clearly.

An item gallery view can be tagged as "NEW", meaning that this slot contains a new entry in its inventory list. The "slot" represents the current index of an inventory list. The conntainer has no inherent notion of "quality" or "level" of other equipment related factors. It's ownn functionaliy can include:
NEW
CALL TO ACTION (add prompt)
LOCKED

The label IS the item. It has a type, it has a name, it can be "equipped" or "assigned"it can have any equipment related property:
EQUIPMENT TYPE
EQUIPMENT NAME
EQUIPMENT LEVEL
EQUIPMENT PROGRESS
IS-EQUIPPED?

housekeeping. removing chaff layers

Its important to state the goal of the Wednesday presentation clearly now while there is time to act with purpose. First of all, you need to recognize that the design has arrived. Beyond the refactoring, the prototyping,m the analysis and all of that, this is the outcome. And its good ennough to work and adapt.
You've been deeply committed to making a model in XD. Even now, you're doing non-design tasks in XD to prep for building out the prototype.
Two things.
1. The prototype is important. Its the value I'm adding. Think of it as a way to (1) generate images for annnotation (2) show examples of states (3) show sequences (4) provide working example.

2. The Packaging is the most important. This is the deliverable. Does it need to be as comprehensively presented as the NAV-ASSIST and QUEST-TRACKER documents? Are those overly detailed?

3. Before beginnning the presentation deck state the MVP requirements for this document clearly. What does it need to do? Who is it for?

2020-11-18 10:27

STATUS

OVERVIEW
PROTOTYPE INTRO
OPENING THE SCREEN
EQUIPMENT LANDING PAGE
LOADOUT PAGE

The Equipment Screen represents equippable game objects visually and statistically. Players use this slot-based screen to manage, modify and compare Weapons, Amps and Armor. Equipment configurations may be stored and recalled as Loadouts.

Goals
provide object-based representations of player-equipment for communicating physicality and progression

How much must a player see before deciding what to do next? Employ progressive disclosure to manage complexity and reveal equipment details on demand

2020-11-19 13:07
meeting went well. Some excellent feedback

1. place Equipment notes in a repeatable array around Player Character, so that you always know that the "slot on the far right" is an AMP slot. MNodern Warefare uses a scheme like this, as does the Division. Tbh those ofther games use that scheme as interactable elememts as well

2. Further work on LOADOUT MENU. Work through some use cases for better insights. At a minimum though show Loadouts as (unsaved, or use color coding to indicate "dirty" state)

3. Sort Mod inventory by Type then Quality
4. Don't hide non-valid MOD types when a slot is assigned to a particular type. Instead, grey out the unassigned contentg and push it to the back of the list

Let me write down my idea for Loadouts before I start working on it.

Loadouts are used to organize equipment assignments

I have 2 loadouts: Red and Blue
For the Red loadout, all my equipment is colored red
For the Blue loadout, all my equipment is colored blue

I begin the game with my Red loadout

I pick up a green item and equip it.
This Modifies my Red Loadout, which is now called Red (Modified)

Why did I go to the Loadout Page?
- I might want to save my new equipment config as a new loadout
- I might want to update current (Red)
- I might want to revert to my Red loadout, where I have all red equipment again

DESIGN
- show "modified" label after loadout name in equipment menu
- on the Loadout screen make the name of the currenty loadout big/obvious
- Menu should read:
	+ FIERCE
		- X: update
		- XX: revert
	+ Save

2020-12-02 10:33
What can you do in the EQUIPMENT prototype.

These are clickthrough prototypes so dynamic state-based behaviors aren't supported. Functionality is limited to areas investigated for the overall design.

Here's what you can do:
- enter the Equipment Screen from PAN-LINK
- step backwards to exit to PAN-LINK
- Page between the LOADOUT SCREEN and the EQUIPMENT SCREEN
- scroll through the list of LOADOUTS to observe keybinds, etc.
- select a LOADOUT to enter the EQUIPMENT SCREEN
- scroll through the Equipment in a LOADOUT
- view details about any Equipment in a LOADOUT
- Open the weapon inventory for the first weapon (Lightning25-22)
- Scroll through the equipment in the weapon inventory
- Compare equipment in the weapon inventory to the equipped item

2020-12-17 08:01
DESCRIBE THE FEATURE IN WORDS

WHAT IS AN EQUIPMENT CONFIGURATION?
All players maintain an equipment configuration composed from gear assignments to equipment slots. This configuration encapsulates any modifications to the assigned gear

WHAT IS IS A LOADOUT?
A loadout is a stored equipment configuration. Imported loadouts replace the existing equipment configuration. Changes to the ongoing state of the equipment configuration do not reflect upon any stored loadouts. However, an equipment configuration can be exported as a loadout for later recall.

WHAT IS AN INVENTORY?
Customer inventory is the collection of all gear, mods and skins being carried at any time.


LOADOUT MANAGEMENT FROM PAN-LINK
The equipment dashboard allows customers to select a loadout in-place, without visiting the equipment screen. Similarly, the equipment configuration can be stored as a loadout directly from the dashboard. The new loadout is automatically named "untitled-xxxx" to avoid data entry. Exiting PAN-LINK, either by continuing to the equipment screen or by returning to the game confirms the loadout selection

WHAT HAPPENS TO GEAR WHEN IT IS NO LONGER EQUIPPED?
Gear customization is persistent. Gear remains customized when not equipped. However gear customization is also dynamic. Customizing one item may change the state of another if a MOD or SKIN needs to be pulled from it. Gear customizations for non-equipped items aren't stored in a loadout, but a customer can reasonably expect the overall state of their inventory to persist across sessions

FILE MANAGEMENT
Loadouts can be created (from the current equipment configuration) overwritten, renamed or deleted as necessary.

IMPORTING LOADOUTS
Importing a loadout replaces the current equipment configuration. When listing the available loadouts, a proxy menu entry titled "Current Gear" is exposed. This way, a customer always has the current equipment configuration available while inspecting other loadouts.

WHAT HAPPENS WHEN YOU SELECT A LOADOUT
Selecting the loadout returns the cutomer to the Equipment Screen. The customer's equipment configuration now matches the configuration defined by the loadout.

MISSING ITEMS
Loadout designations are incomplete when they no longer match the customer inventory. The missing items (Equipment, Mods, Skins) may be STASHED or UNAVAILABLE

When an incomplete loadout is imported the Equipment Configuration is updated with all valid items. Missing items will be ignored.

When the missing items are stashed the customer is notified that these items are available in their stash

When the missing items are unavailable the customer is notified that these items no longer exist. A prompt is provided to "cleanup" which will remove the missing items and update the loadout.

KEYBINDS
Buttons are held (long press) to confirm a destructive action
- importing a loadout
- overwriting a loadout
- creating a new loadoutg
- removing a loadout
- cleaning up a loadout

Buttons are tapped (pressed) to initiate other functions
- renaming a loadout
- duplicating a loadout
- comparing loadouts

2020-12-24 16:22
finished mapping out the design brief. I can answer some of the questions I posed earlier.

WHAT DOES A SKIN ITEM LOOK LIKE?
Skins are presented as app icons in a gallery list. Visuals are constructed from a stack of layered content allowing for modular and thematic representations.

WHAT DOES A SKIN PROFILE LOOK LIKE?
The Skin profile shows Skin name, condition, appraised value, quality, edition, description and an array of implemented visual components. Presentation extends MOD Profile

HOW DO YOU SHOW SKIN ASSIGNMENTS ON EQUIPMENT NOTES?
The Skin's Appicon is prominently shown, similarly to MODs, but with larger visual identity

HOW DO YOU SHOW SKIN ASSIGNMENTS ON EQUIPMENT PROFILE?
(assumption)
Create a dedicated Profile row
will need to show appicon, condition, quality

HOW DO YOU ASSIGN SKINS TO EQUIPPED ITEMS?
(assumption)
workflow should be identical to MOD assignment
Can this workflow be integrated into MOD assignment as an additional type of MOD slot?

HOW DO YOU SHOW AN INVENTORY OF SKINS?
(keep thinking about it)
What's important to a customer when they're looking through a Skin inventory?
How did they arrive at this moment?
Potential point of purchase. I think its important to call out sales and marketing opportunity for game content here

2021-01-11 06:03
Matt F
matt.firor@zenimaxonline.com

ESO IN 2021
Due to a variety of issues, including work from home, a very aggressive development schedule, chronic understaffing, and lack of QA resources, ESO's quality has slipped alarmingly. This cannot be allowed to continue in 2021 - additional staff has been approved to help shore up our dev team so we can work smarter and better and be able to fix long-standing issues and ensure that we do not introduce additional problems. With this in mind, we are purposefully focusing on bugs and performance issues over new features next year. We will have a couple of hallmark new features in 2021 (Companions, revamped Champion Point system), but other than those, we need to take the time and make the game perform better and not be so unstable.

The ESO BMT is working on a plan to greatly increase ESO's profile in the industry next year, with a campaign focused around Project Osprey (ESO next-gen) launch, E3, and our 2021 Chapter, Blackwood. This moment in time, in June, hopefully with a big stage presence, should go a long way to raise the media and industry's perception of ESO. We have already filmed the "virtual 2021 announce" show for our Gates of Oblivion content, and of course you all hopefully saw the amazing teaser trailer we debuted at The Game Awards a few days ago. 2021 will be a big year for us, and we are always well underway with plans to support it.

I have had a couple of opportunities to get a hands-on demo of Osprey running, and it is very cool. Essentially it is like ESO on a high-end gaming PC, but on a console. It looks and feels amazing. All three next-gen platforms will have user-selectable "Fidelity" and "Performance" settings so they can choose whether they would like a higher-fidelity or a higher-framerate experience.

SPG IN 2021
 We now have a functioning technical proof-of-concept for the ESO Companion App, and we've already approved the next set of features to be supported by it, as well as the next in-app game to help the Companion App gain some traction. I'm looking forward to see both substantially evolve over the next year and get the app in the hands of employees for testing.


2021 HIRING
We managed to hire over 50 new FTEs in 2020, even with the many hurdles the year threw at us. In 2021, we'll be ramping that up significantly, as we grow the Kestrel team in anticipation of production, and of course to support ESO with the much-needed staff it needs to keep it on the rails and moving forward. The hiring plans are aggressive, but it is nothing we haven't done before. By the end of 2021, we should have ESO staffed for (more) long-term success, and be well on the road to getting Kestrel into production in June 2022. Additionally, we’ll be getting our satellite studios off the ground in 2021, proving the concept, and moving forward.


How do various ZOS development plans for the next year align with my goals anbd skills?
The question isn't "do I still fit?" Its much broader

- Is there an opportunity to do work that I haven't done before?
- Is there an opportunity to revisit the work I have done before?
- Is there an opportunity for a new role at the studio? If so, how would I pitch that? What needs are being overlooked?
- Where does the studio value my creative spark?

2021-01-13 22:02

Hi Matt, after reading your recent email about plans for increasing ESO's industry profile this year, I began considering how to help. I also read your concerns about product quality and understandably, the devteam is prioritizing systemic bug fixes and performance issues right now.

I love ESO, and want to make it better. Always have. Without knowing what plans are in place, I can only suggest broad strokes for product improvement.

- Better accomodation for 4K resolution (layout and text formatting refinement)
- More simple beauty (the button styling and other storytelling we designed for elderscrollsonline.com is a good example of this principle)
- Improved presentation for high engagement systems (Crafting, Skills, Champion Points)
- Improved customer experience for MTX features

It's often surprising to recognize how much production goes on at the studio. I'm personally familiar with certain areas, such as ESO, KESTREL, Platform Services and Marketing. Its especially gratifying to see how these silos can feed into one another, for example - using ESO-UI for KESTREL prototyping. With regard to the growing ESO ecosystem, I think there's space for discussing how the parts can best complement one another - companion app, web platform, MTX, Gameplay, Sales funnel, Onboarding, etc...

Anyway Matt, those are my thoughts. I'd welcome the chance to share more. Let's keep working together.

Hi Matt, 

After reading your recent email regarding  ESO's increased industry profile this year, I began considering how to help. The game has a great head start in the nextgen marketplace. It’s become quite an ecosystem over the last several years. Customers have felt things, achieved things in this space and expanded upon it. What a goddamn product! 
I also read your concerns about product quality and understandably, the devteam is prioritizing systemic bug fixes and performance issues right now.

I love ESO, and want to make it better. Always have. The attached graph (career growth over time) shows that this growth has been mutual. 
Without knowing what plans are in place, I can only suggest broad strokes for product improvement. I’d be interested in making the conversation more specific though. 

- Better accomodation for 4K resolution (layout and text formatting refinement)
- More simple beauty (the button styling and other storytelling we designed for elderscrollsonline.com is a good example of this principle)
- Improved presentation for high engagement systems (Crafting, Skills, Champion Points)
- Improved customer experience for MTX features

It's often surprising to recognize how much production goes on at ZOS. I'm intimately familiar with certain silos, such as ESO, KESTREL, Platform Services and Marketing, but there’s more going on than I know about. 
Anyway Matt, those are my thoughts. I welcome the chance to speak further. You know what I’m capable of; you’ve helped me get there, after all.


2021-01-15 14:17
candidate:

Hi Matt,

I read your recent email describing ESO's positioning for 2021 and want to share some thoughts. I love ESO and want to help it thrive. Always have. The attached graph of my career growth over time shows this has been mutual.

A few broad strokes. ESO-UI functions well (better than I imagined) however I believe a tactical visual refresh can help legitimize and support the industry positioning you spoke about. For example, here are some casual observations from watching others play.

- Product front-end needs to accommodate 4K more effectively (1080p was the market norm at design time) 

- Product front-end can incorporate more simple beauty (button styling and other storytelling we designed for elderscrollsonline.com is a good example of incorporating this principle into agile dev)

- Presentation and UX for progression-based systems (Crafting, Skills, Champion Points) is sort of heavy and in some cases, complicated. 
- Crown Store purchases can be further expanded out of the Storefront (I’ve examined this at length and call the principle “purchase at the point of friction”)
- “What should we do next?” is an interesting dynamic while playing in a group. I believe there are further use cases to be explored here
It's often surprising to recognize how much production goes on at ZOS. I'm intimately familiar with certain silos, such as ESO, KESTREL, Platform Services and Marketing, but there’s a lot more going on than I know about. 

Anyway Matt, those are my thoughts. I welcome the chance to speak further. You know what I’m capable of; after all, you helped me get there. 



2021-01-15 14:32
final

Hi Matt,

I read your recent email describing ESO's positioning for 2021. Let me share some thoughts with you. I love ESO and want to help it thrive. Always have. The attached graph of my career growth over time shows this has been mutual.

A few broad strokes.

ESO-UI functions well (better than I imagined.) A visual refresh can help legitimize and support the industry positioning you spoke about. For example, here are some casual observations from watching others play.

- Product front-end styling needs to accommodate 4K more effectively. (1080p was the market norm at design time)

- Product front-end styling can incorporate more simple beauty. Button styling and other storytelling we designed for elderscrollsonline.com is a good example of this.

- UI Presentation for progression-based systems (Crafting, Skills, Champion Points) is heavy. These systems do a lot at once. The work I did on Kestrel suggested other possibilities to me.

- Crown Store purchases can be further expanded out of the Storefront. (I’ve examined this at length and call the principle “purchase at the point of friction”)

- “What should we do next?” is an interesting dynamic while playing in a group. I believe there are further use cases to be explored here

It's often surprising to recognize how much production goes on at ZOS. I'm familiar with certain silos, such as ESO, KESTREL, Platform Services and Marketing, but there’s a lot more going on than I know about.

Anyway, Matt, those are my thoughts. I welcome the chance to speak further. You know what I’m capable of; after all, you helped me get there.

	Let me think about this, though. We have a ton of other more pressing issues we need to address first, but ping me in a month or so when hopefully I have some clear air and we can talk about this.
